issue_title: UHD Receive Stream for large sample rate/ bw causing SEGV
issue_body:
## Expected Behavior
  * Request large-ish sample rate or bandwidth from spectrum analyzer 

## Actual Behavior
  * Python crash system alert (without crashing URH)

## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

  1. Open URH (verbose used after crash discovered)
      * python3 -vv /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/bin/urh >> urh.py.log 2>&1
  2. File -> Spectrum Analyzer
      * Device USRP (installed and enabled under Help -> Options -> Device, status OK)
      * Freq Arbitrary, e.x. 433.920M
      * Sample Rate: 20.000M (device rating 61.44MSps)
      * Bandwidth: 20.000M (device rating 56MHz)
      * Gain: Arbitrary, e.x. 20
  3. Click Start
  4. Log window shows:
USRP-OPEN: Success
USRP: Single USRP:
  Device: B-Series Device
  Mboard 0: B200
  RX Channel: 0
    RX DSP: 0
    RX Dboard: A
    RX Subdev: FE-RX1
  TX Channel: 0
    TX DSP: 0
    TX Dboard: A
    TX Subdev: FE-TX1
USRP-SET_FREQUENCY to 433920000.0: Success
USRP-SET_SAMPLE_RATE to 20000000.0: Success
USRP-SET_BANDWIDTH to 20000000.0: Success
USRP-SET_RF_GAIN to 0.2: Success
USRP: Initializing stream...
USRP: Initialized stream

  5. Somewhere during the USRP-SET actions the system launches a crash report for Python
      * __pyx_pw_3src_3urh_3dev_6native_3lib_4usrp_17recv_stream causing _platform_memmove causing segmentation violation
      * [syscrash.log](https://github.com/jopohl/urh/files/1341652/syscrash.log)
  6. Freqency graph stops updating (no recv)
  7. URH Log does not show error
      * [urh.py.log](https://github.com/jopohl/urh/files/1341655/urh.py.log)

## Platform Specifications

  - Python Version: 3.6.2
  - Operating System:  Mac OS X 10.12.1 (16B2657)
  - Memory: 32GB
  - Version of URH: 1.8.7
  - URH was installed: __via pip__ 
  - SDR: Ettus B200 (over USB3.0)

issue_title: TextRank算法结果没有过滤停止词?
issue_body:
我在不少文章上进行了测试,大部分情况下, Textrank算法的效果不错, 但是在某些情况下, Textrank算法会导出大量的停止词. 我还没读那篇paper, 所以具体原因也不是很明白. 提供一个样例: 
text = """
2015全球移动互联网大会期间，专注人机交互解决方案的Synaptics公司在北京举行了媒体见面会，Synaptics独立触控业务部副总裁Brian Daly在会后接受了媒体采访，作为唯一一名受邀出席的自媒体人，我有幸与Brian Daly就指纹识别技术、网络信息安全、智能汽车等领域交换了各自的看法。 Brian Daly在演讲中提到了触控技术为人机交互带来的变革，以及未来人机交互的发展趋势。其中从按键到触屏的转变是交互方式变革的关键，生物识别、情境识别以及全方位感知技术将在未来应用与各种硬件设备和现实生活场景中，而传统的人机交互方式将逐渐退出历史舞台。 一个十分有趣的现象是，从年龄上看比我们更早接触触屏设备的儿童，在使用触屏手机、平板电脑等产品后，会误认为电视机、电冰箱的屏幕同样支持触摸操作，当他的指令得不到设备反馈是，他会认为这台设备是坏的。这一现象或许可以说明传统的人机交互技术已经无法满足人们的生活需求，而在智能家居、可穿戴设备、智能汽车等领域，触控技术仍大有可为。 Synaptics公司总裁兼首席执行官 Rick Bergman向观众演示了触控技术为智能汽车提供的人机交互解决方案。汽车的前窗玻璃成了一块儿可触摸操作的屏幕，在行驶过程中驾驶员可点击屏幕图标进行导航、查询路况等操作，不过对于直接关系到人身安全的汽车而言，人们依然有许多不安和疑惑，比如如何避免误操作？Brian Daly在采访中表示不同功能会配合不同的震动频率以确保触控的准确性。 除了在更多设备和生活场景中的应用外，人们对触控技术的需求还体现在操作的灵敏程度和操作前的预览功能上。比如，带着手套也能实现触控。在滑雪、打高尔夫、开车等生活场景中用户需要带手套，触控技术的创新将能够满足用户在这方面的需求。另外在手指潮湿或干燥的情况下，同样也能够实现触控。第三是能够在用户做出选择前进行预览，比如绽放图片和地图。 有媒体记者提问：“为什么我的iPhone屏幕在沾到水的情况下无法触控？”Brian Daly笑道：“那是因为iPhone没有采用Synaptics的解决方案。” Synaptics除了为各大OEM厂商提供人机交互解决方案外，还有一个身份是“FIDO联盟”的核心成员。该联盟是一个制定生物识别认证系统标准的国际联盟，其口号是“杀死密码”。不过，我对FIDO这一宏伟的目标表示质疑，Brian Daly在这一问题上的表现出的诚恳让我感到敬佩。以下是对话实录： ME：Brian Daly先生，关于指纹识别技术，我有两个问题，一个小问题，一个大问题。小问题是我对FIDO提出的“杀死密码”的口号有些疑虑，我认为在未来很长一段时间内指纹识别技术仍然无法得以普及，最乐观的情况是与密码方式并存。因为你无法回避一些相对极端的案例，比如我的手指受伤了、烧伤了、蜕皮了等情况，如果我完全使用指纹识别的话，将无法访问我所有的账户和设备。不知道您对我的观点怎么看？ Brian Daly：我同意生物识别技术包括指纹识别技术确实在今后一段时间内将会与传统密码技术共存的。因为即便是手指太干（或太湿）的情况下，触控技术都会受到影响，更别说受伤和蜕皮的情况了。所以必须要有另外一些不同的生物身份识别的方式作为补充，比如面部识别、虹膜识别，甚至是个人签字也可以，还有各种不同的方法来帮助你，一种方法不行了，马上转到另外一个。另外密码识别的方法还是一种非常好的备份。 ME：继续刚才的问题。我的大问题是，我们知道网络安全问题目前已经上升到国家战略层面，Synaptics的触控技术，特别是指纹识别技术是一项涉及到用户隐私数据的技术，而FIDO结合Synaptics指纹识别技术将要制定的生物识别认证系统国际标准，在与不同国家政府间的合作的过程中会否因为信息安全问题遭到阻力？同时作为一个成立于美国的联盟，FIDO如何证明它的独立性？ Brian Daly：这个确实牵扯的方面太多了，作为一家公司或者一个联盟的成员，我们应该以全球化的思维方式来思考问题，我们确实跟世界各国政府和地区都有合作，而网络安全问题对我们而言确实也是一个挑战。但对Synaptics公司来讲，我们在这方面是有优势的，我们不仅面向全球，我们在世界各国本地都有很多本地化的工作。在中国我们业务量就非常大，我们跟中国政府和一些企业部门也合作得非常好。 ME：刚才在会场看到Rick演示的人机交互技术在智能汽车上的应用，我感到很惊讶。我们知道人是经常犯错误的，但人是不允许机器犯错的，特别是汽车这种直接关系到人生命安全的交通工具，所以人们对安全的担忧，会不会使得这项更前卫的人机交互技术，包括现在的无人驾驶技术推行起来有很大的阻力？ Brian Daly：关于您提到的无人驾驶汽车，我认为还处在非常早的发展时期。但是美国确实已经出现这种无人驾驶车，横跨美国行驶过。虽然能做到这样，美国只有少量几个州允许无人驾驶汽车能够上路行驶。很多州是不允许车上没人的，必须有一个司机控制这个车。但是我认为这项技术早晚还会被真正采用，但现在还是早期。目前已经有这样的技术，比如汽车可以在你的车逼近另外一个车之前自动感知到，能够帮助你自动制动、刹车。我认为你刚才讲的无人驾驶汽车这个技术，还要花很多年才能真正得到市场的采用。 ME：刚才Rick分享了人机交互技术的四次变革，其中有一个是情境识别，我不是特别理解，能否举一个简单典型的例子，或者现在已经应用到了一些设备上的案例？ Brian Daly：我可以举个例子，比如现在在美国很多用户都使用过Google now这个应用，它可以知道你的地点，我一到北京这个软件就知道我已经从美国到了中国北京了，附近有一个水族中心等等，水立方那儿不是有个水族中心吗，它会识别你可能到中国会有旅游的需求，就马上推出一些新的建议和想法，比如在周围应该看一些什么景点。或者到了该吃饭的时间，手机就感知到我现在在什么位置，就告诉我在北京什么区域，这些地方有些什么样的饭馆，给你提一些建议。还有比如我在美国要赶飞机，手机就感知到了我要坐飞机，而且知道机场在什么地方，而且还知道我现在在什么位置上，还知道交通堵不堵。如果堵的话，会提醒我早点走。如果飞机延误了，它会提前告诉我你不用着急那么走了。这些就是情境感知的应用。作者微信：375952010，扫一扫关注作者公众号。
"""

rtn = jieba.analyse.textrank(text)
print (text)
rtn2 = jieba.analyse.extract_tags(text)
type(rtn)

print (','.join(rtn2))

issue_title: Improve on-retry logging
issue_body:
Hi!

And thank you for forking the one and only library for better retries in Python. Tenacity API looks better, although I'm still to find an opportunity to use it in my projects.

More often than not, if there's a retry, I'd like it logged somewhere. With `retrying` I found myself using `retry_on_exception` functions that contained log messages with smth like
```
X failed, retrying (maybe): <error message>
```
because `retry_on_exception` function doesn't know whether or not there will be another retry attempt.

tenacity makes a step in the right direction with `after=after_log` function, but it's kind of lacking because different bits of information that can be useful to log are available in different places, such as:
- first attempt start time or total time elapsed since then: available to `stop` functions
- next attempt delay: available as a return value of  `wait` functions
- attempt number: available to all (?) but `retry` functions
- invalid value returned or exception raised: available to `retry` functions

Providing just one logger/level/msgformat combination might not be enough as the user could want to use different levels for different situations, including:
- reporting retried attempts as WARNING and the last one as ERROR
- using different loglevels for different errors
- provide more information about the error in the log where possible

So I presume it should be another kwarg that accepts a function, e.g. `log_retry=` or `on_retry=`. And that function could accept an `attempt` with all interesting metadata attached (right now only `attempt_number` is attached to the `Future` instance), so as to avoid function signature issues when available metadata is altered.

issue_title: Collinearity mitigation on StackingClassifier and StackingCVClassifier
issue_body:
Issue:

Arises when you implement / execute stacking on a dataset `D` by scoring the dataset using the `predict_proba` or `predict` methods for each 'input' model, then concatenate the scores as e.g.: `D' = concatenate([model.predict_proba(D) for model in base_models], axis=1)`.

Problem: since each matrix `model.predict_proba(D)` (for each `model`) satisfies `sum(model.predict_proba(D), axis=1) == np.ones(..)`, the resulting collinearity in the matrix `D'` may affect convergence or parameter estimates esp. when the stacking metaestimator is a regression model.

Propose:

Add an option to score the dataset as follows:

`D_ = concatenate([model.predict_proba(D)[:, -1] for model in base_models], axis=1)`.

issue_title: KWallet always opened at import when available
issue_body:
When KWallet is available it is always opened on import of keyring, even if the configuration is set to use another backend.

Therfore you are prompted for the KWallet password even if you don't use it.

Also when GnomeKeyring and KWallet are both available on a System, GnomeKeyring is used per default even within a KDE session.

I've created a fork which fixes this:
https://bitbucket.org/sutupud/python-keyring-lib

regards,
sutu

issue_title: keyring leaks password into process table on OS X
issue_body:
Based on casual reading of the code in keyring/backends/OS_X.py, this code shells out to security(1), passing it the '-w password' option.  This leaks the password into the process table.

Unfortunately, security(1) does not provide any alternative methods of supplying the password, but it's possible to use a hack around it by creating a fifo, writing the password into the fifo and then using the shell's redirection mechanism to get input from the fifo to the command.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/kang/python-keyring-lib/issue/150
- Originally reported by: Anonymous
- Originally created at: 2014-09-10T02:53:29.177

```
def __init__(self):
    self.params = [spotpy.parameter.List('x',[1,2,3,4,6,7,8,9,0]), #Give possible x values as a List
                   spotpy.parameter.List('y',[0,1,2,5,7,8,9,0,1])]  #Give possible y values as a List

issue_title: Python 3 support - 'wb' write mode causes TypeError
issue_body:
I'm trying to use Placebo in Python 3, and getting an exception `TypeError: a bytes-like object is required, not 'str'` when trying to save the API calls.

The issue is with the save method, line 60 of placebo.py: 
```python
    def save(self, path):
        with open(path, 'wb') as fp:
            json.dump(self._mock_responses, fp, indent=4)
```

The target file is being written as binary. In Python2 8-bit strings also handle byte sequences, but in Python3 byte sequences have their own `bytes` type, causing the TypeError. (https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0404/#strings-and-bytes)

@garnaat Was there a specific reason for the binary mode on the `save()` and `load()` methods? If not, the easiest fix is probably to just change them to 'r' and 'w'.

issue_title: Problem with boto3
issue_body:
This is most likely related to #247 - I've cornered where the issue is happening, but I'm unsure as to how I'd fix this problem. When using vcr.py with a pretty normal boto3 use-case (reading from s3) I stumbled upon this exception : 

  File "[...]/lib/python3.4/site-packages/botocore/vendored/requests/packages/urllib3/connection.py", line 234, in connect
    self.sock = ssl_wrap_socket(conn, self.key_file, self.cert_file,
AttributeError: 'AWSHTTPSConnection' object has no attribute 'key_file'

It turns out that a certain check is failing : line 700 in botocore/vendored/requests/packages/urllib3/connectionpool.py

if isinstance(conn, VerifiedHTTPSConnection):

This line failing implies the "set_cert()" method is not called, which causes the aforementioned exception. Using pdb I can see that VerifiedHTTPSConnection is patched with vcr.py and that this is most likely why the isinstance() check fails.

Anyone can reproduce this issue? Here are the versions I'm using : 

vcrpy==1.10.1
boto3==1.4.0
botocore==1.4.53

issue_title: non unicode data not correctly handled in Python 3
issue_body: Filenames and command line arguments can include (non-unicode) binary data on most operating systems.  This is more difficult to handle on Python 3 because these are generally expressed as the str type.  I've created pull request https://github.com/giampaolo/psutil/pull/733 with a test case that highlights the issue but do not have a solution at this point.

issue_title: Add documentation on how to setup AWS IAM
issue_body:
It took me some time to find out on how to setup AWS IAM user/role/policy. Maybe it's helpful for other users on how to get started.

I created a user `keymaker` and create a policy to limit only listing users/groups and ssh keys:

    {
        "Version": "2012-10-17",
        "Statement": [
            {
                "Sid": "AllowKeymakerToListAccounts",
                "Effect": "Allow",
                "Action": [
                    "iam:ListAccountAliases",
                    "iam:ListUsers",
                    "iam:ListGroups",
                    "iam:ListGroupsForUser",
                    "iam:GetUser",
                    "iam:GetGroup",
                    "iam:ListSSHPublicKeys",
                    "iam:GetSSHPublicKey"
                ],
                "Resource": "*"
            }
        ]
    }

To have the user automatically added to IAM groups the group name must be prepended with `keymaker_` AND must have the path `/keymaker/`. However it is not possible to create a group from Amazon AWS console and define the path, you must use CLI:

    $ aws iam create-group --path "/keymaker/" --group-name keymaker_mylinuxgroup

Notes:
- Currently you must duplicate the groups in IAM: one with dash, one with underscore. See issue https://github.com/kislyuk/keymaker/issues/10
- All existing linux users not in the defined in the IAM keymaker group will be removed. See issue https://github.com/kislyuk/keymaker/issues/11

issue_title: retry_times set to 0 always fails to acquire a lock
issue_body:
I recently tried setting retry_times to 0 to have RedLock not retry on its own and my locks always failed to acquire. I realized that this option must be at least 1 as it is not the number of retries but the number of tries.

I suggest either changing the loop for acquiring to add 1 to retry_times to make the name make more sense or to raise an exception if retry_times is <= 0 in RedLock.__init__() so that this incorrect condition cannot happen.

container for disposal according to local regulations (see section 13). Keep in suitable, closed containers for disposal.", on page 2 of the pdf file. The problem is that for this particular pdf file, my code will also extract "Product This combustible material may be burned in a chemical incinerator equipped with an afterburner and scrubber. Offer surplus and non-recyclable solutions to a licensed disposal company." on p.5.  Because I want to work with many pdf files that might have "6.3..." content on different page, I figure if I can pass the pageid in the extract then it should be fine.

issue_title: Setting dynamically generated key of arbitrary depth
issue_body:
How to assign dynamically generated key, value pairs using the provided API?

Suppose, I have a key of format `field1.field2.field3...` but whose depth is known only at runtime. How to assign such a key value pair?

I ideally would like to be able to do something like this
```
config['window.title'] = value
```
Both the suggested options are not very helpful because

1. `config.window.title`  requires the strings `window` and `title` to be known at the time of code writing. However, in my use case, they are known only at runtime.
2. `config['window']['title']` requires the depth of key to be known at the time of code writing. However, for my use case, the depth is known only at runtime.

The provided API looks to be too restrictive and insufficient for the very basic case of assigning value to a key generated at runtime.

issue_title: canplayer - stream captured frames to virtual device
issue_body:
While capturing a db file is useful for offline analysis it can be very useful to "replay" a captured session.

This issue is to create such a command line tool that works with our supported formats - at least the `.db`.

Possible flags (from `canplayer`):
```
Usage can_player.py <options> input-file
-c CAN channel
-i python-can interface
-t ignore timestamps (send immediately at minimum gap)
-g <ms> minimum gap between sent frames
-s <s> skip gaps greater than 's' seconds
-v print sent CAN frames
```

issue_title: Option to color nodes based on time spent in function rather than whole subtree
issue_body:
This is a feature request to add a flag to color graph nodes based on time spent in each function, rather than coloring based on time spend in _the whole subtree_.

Concrete example (from https://github.com/jrfonseca/gprof2dot/blob/master/sample.png): `main` is colored red (since it's the call tree root), yet it's likely not a good place to look for optimizations. Same goes with small functions on top of expensive calls (`read_all_makefiles`). They should probably not be colored "hot" if they're fast. The expensive function call should!

I will look into implementing this soon if I have some spare time.

issue_title: Tests fail due to unified_diff output difference in 2.7.10, 3.2
issue_body:
The unified_diff() header usually doesn't include the count when there is only one line. The tests assume this to be true when testing for `@@ -1 +1 @@` as part of the CLI output.

For some reason in 2.7.10 and 3.2 specifically, the unified_diff output includes the count (`@@ -1,1 +1,1 @@`). This doesn't appear to occur in <2.7.9 or >3.2.

I haven't found a changelog for these python versions that describes this behavior. In regards to a fix would your preference be to attempt to force the output to match across all versions, or attempt to make the tests less dependent on matching this string?

issue_title: No cast remote control notification when casting was started by pychromecast
issue_body:
A few months ago, google enabled so called "cast remote control notifications" on android devices. (https://support.google.com/chromecast/answer/7206638?hl=en)

In short: all android devices on the same network get an notification to control the chromecast when playing, even if they dont started the cast session by themselves. Which is very convenient because i can stop the stream from any device.

Dont know if its a bug or even related directly with pychromecast, but I dont get the remote control notifications on any of my android devices if the cast was started by a pychromecast script.

Besides that, casting works nicely and also the chromecast/home app on android shows the chromecast as playing.

In all other cases (started by android device - different apps - casting via chrome from same PC as pychromcast) the notification shows up on all my android devices.

Has someone else observed this? Any other idea what could cause the different behaviour?

issue_title: Ease declaration of items in fixed order
issue_body:
User wants to be able to predict the order in which `config.iter_items` returns items within a section. That is impossible if config declaration is specified in a plain `dict`. Current workaround is to use an `OrderedDict`, but that makes it less readable:

```
config = Config(collections.OrderedDict([
    ('comment', Item()),  # this is essentially the same as 'comment': NotSet
    ('description', ''),
    ('enabled', False),
    ('threads', Item(value=5)),
]))
```

**Better**:

```
config = Config([
    Item('comment'),
    ('description', ''),
    ('enabled', False),
    Item('threads', value=5),
])
```

issue_title: Items mistakenly shown as used
issue_body:
Vulture looks great! I'm evaluating it to use on a large code base.

            lock_time = self._read_le_uint32()
            expiry = self._read_le_uint32()

        if txType != 1:    
            witnesses = self._read_witnesses(txType)

        return TxDecred(
            version,
            inputs,
            outputs,
            lock_time,
            expiry,
            witnesses
        )    


    def _read_witnesses(self, txType):
        read_witness = self._read_witness
        return [read_witness(txType) for i in range(self._read_varint())]

    def _read_witness(self, txType):

        value = ''
        blockHeight = ''
        blockIndex = ''
        script = ''

        if txType == 0 or txType == 2: 
            value = self._read_le_int64()
            blockHeight = self._read_le_uint32()
            blockIndex = self._read_le_uint32()
            script = self._read_varbytes()
        if txType == 3:
            script = self._read_varbytes()
        if txType == 4:
            value = self._read_le_int64()
            script = self._read_varbytes()

        return TxDecredWitness(
            value,
            blockHeight,
            blockIndex,
            script
        )

    def _read_inputs(self):
        read_input = self._read_input
        return [read_input() for i in range(self._read_varint())]

    def _read_input(self):
        return TxDecredInput(
            self._read_nbytes(32),  #prev_hash
            self._read_le_uint32(), #prev_idx
            self._read_byte(),       #tree
            self._read_le_uint32() #sequence
        )
    
    def _read_outputs(self):
        read_output = self._read_output
        return [read_output() for i in range(self._read_varint())]

    def _read_output(self):
        return TxDecredOutput(
            self._read_le_int64(),                   #value
            self._read_le_uint16(),                  #version
            self._read_varbytes(),  #script
        )

    def read_header(self, height, static_header_size):
        '''Return the block header bytes'''
        return self._read_nbytes(static_header_size) 
```

issue_title: Subtract characters
issue_body:
Below a feature suggestion path.

The number of operations specified with -s leads to very different vocabulary sizes, due to the number of unique characters to start with. A value of 49500 creates small vocabularies for language that use Latin alphabet, but easily 70000 or so for Chinese. So, it would be good to subtract the number of unique characters from the number of symbols being generated. 

+++ b/subword_nmt/learn_bpe.py
@@ -56,6 +56,9 @@ def create_parser(subparsers=None):
     parser.add_argument('--dict-input', action="store_true",
         help="If set, input file is interpreted as a dictionary where each line contains a word-count pair")
     parser.add_argument(
+        '--total-symbols', '-t', action="store_true",
+        help="subtract number of characters from the symbols to be generated")
+    parser.add_argument(
         '--verbose', '-v', action="store_true",
         help="verbose mode.")

@@ -197,7 +200,7 @@ def prune_stats(stats, big_stats, threshold):
                 big_stats[item] = freq


-def learn_bpe(infile, outfile, num_symbols, min_frequency=2, verbose=False, is_dict=False):
+def learn_bpe(infile, outfile, num_symbols, min_frequency=2, verbose=False, is_dict=False, total_symbols=False):
     """Learn num_symbols BPE operations from vocabulary, and write to outfile.
     """

@@ -211,6 +214,16 @@ def learn_bpe(infile, outfile, num_symbols, min_frequency=2, verbose=False, is_d

     stats, indices = get_pair_statistics(sorted_vocab)
     big_stats = copy.deepcopy(stats)
+
+    if total_symbols:
+        uniq_char = defaultdict(int)
+        for word in vocab:
+            prev_char = word[0]
+            for char in word[1:]:
+                uniq_char[char] += 1
+        print('Number of characters: {0}'.format(len(uniq_char)))
+        num_symbols -= len(uniq_char)
+
     # threshold is inspired by Zipfian assumption, but should only affect speed
     threshold = max(stats.values()) / 10
     for i in range(num_symbols):
@@ -270,4 +283,4 @@ if __name__ == '__main__':
     if args.output.name != '<stdout>':
         args.output = codecs.open(args.output.name, 'w', encoding='utf-8')

-    learn_bpe(args.input, args.output, args.symbols, args.min_frequency, args.verbose, is_dict=args.dict_input)

I first thought it could be the order in which the tests are run but -v shows me the same order every time, but not the same function being the "outlier".  

I guess this could be an issue with the OS and that I shouldn't expect this to be a benchmark since the tests are only being run once, but I'm puzzled as to why it is that might be causing the problem.

Below is the sample code/unit test and the results of some runs.

```python
import unittest
'''
Simple sample function:

get multiply all items in a vector by x,
square them and provide result 

The goal is to show how one test, 
the one that is probably run first,
seems to take longer than the rest.
'''


def one(vec, x):
    return list(map(lambda c: (x*c)**2, vec))


def one_clone(vec, x):
    return list(map(lambda c: (x*c)**2, vec))


def two(vec, x):
    resvec = []
    for v in vec:
        resvec.append((v * x)**2)
    return resvec


def two_clone(vec, x):
    resvec = []
    for v in vec:
        resvec.append((v * x)**2)
    return resvec


class TestNoseTimer(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.vec = list(range(5))
        self.factor = 5
        self.expected = [0, 25, 100, 225, 400]

    def test_two_clone(self):
        result = two_clone(self.vec, self.factor)
        self.assertEquals(result, self.expected)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

issue_body:
Is there a way merge strategy can be applied to include?  When executing the following code:

```
import anyconfig
f1 = anyconfig.load("a.yml", ac_template=True)
f2 = anyconfig.load("b.yml", ac_template=True)

anyconfig.merge(f1, f2, ac_merge=anyconfig.MS_DICTS)
print f1
```

### a.yml

```
{% include 'included.yml' %}
a: 1
b:
   - c: 3
d:
   e: "bbb"
```

### b.yml

```
---
b:
   - c: 0
   - c: 2
d:
   e: "aaa"
   f: 3
```

The result should be:

`{'a': 1, 'b': [{'c': 3}], 'd': {'e': "bbb", 'f': 3}}`

issue_title: Diff Cover Needs Command line switch to declare encoding of source files
issue_body:
https://github.com/Bachmann1234/diff-cover/pull/60#issuecomment-292097021

issue_title: Library is susceptible to XML Injection
issue_body:
Hi,

the library fails to XML-encode the dict input and is therefore susceptible to XML injection. See this example:

```
>>> from dicttoxml import dicttoxml
>>> dict = {'foo': 'bar', '" injected-attribute="value" <!--': 'bar'}
>>> print(dicttoxml(dict, attr_type=False))
b'<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?><root><key name="" injected-attribute="value" <!--">bar</key><foo>bar</foo></root>'
```

So one can change the XML structure. Suppose the application using this library and the input comes (at least partially) from the user, then this may lead to severe security vulnerabilities. See also [here](https://www.owasp.org/index.php/Testing_for_XML_Injection_(OTG-INPVAL-008)).

Proposed solution: XML-encode all data that is placed into the XML document.

Cheers
Thomas

Feature request: Show current wall time instead of elapsed time
issue_body:
#### Description

For long running jobs where individual tasks may take a long time or stall, `Elapsed Time` becomes hard to interpret. What would be more useful for me is a `Current Time` indicator. This would allow me, at a glance, to see if the jobs are still completing, or if they have stalled and haven't made progress in hours.

#### Code

One possible interface:
```py
for job in ProgressBar()(create_jobs(), current_time=True):
   ...
```

which would output:
```
...
  1% (177 of 10001) |              | Current Time: 16:39:23 ETA: 1 day, 12:25:58
  1% (178 of 10001) |              | Current Time: 16:39:23 ETA: 1 day, 12:13:50
  1% (179 of 10001) |              | Current Time: 16:39:49 ETA: 1 day, 12:25:10
```

issue_title: Multiple LocalForward options
issue_body:
I would like to open 2 TCP tunnels when connected to a single host.

Using configuration like:
```
hosts:
  myhost:
    HostName: 1.2.3.4
    LocalForward: "3307 127.0.0.1:3306"
    LocalForward: "8080 10.1.2.3:80"
```

is not supported and silently (!) only uses the last `LocalForward` option.

It would be great if multiple forwards are accepted like (option *a*):
```
    LocalForward:
      - "3307 127.0.0.1:3306"
      - "8080 10.1.2.3:80"
```

I personally would like a configuration like (option *b*):
```
    LocalForward:
      3307: "127.0.0.1:3306"
      8080: "10.1.2.3:80"
```

(because a mapping still allows for inheritance and overriding specific keys)

issue_title: How to set background color of a screen object
issue_body:
Ok, so I might be overlooking this, but I am not able to find it in the documentation nor by reviewing the source code.

I am trying to create a simple menu with a bunch of options which I will then select using it's corresponding number. That will be captured by the keyboard. At the moment I try to set a fixed color for the background. (black) 

For this I do not feel I need scenes, but it seems like I am not able to manipulate the screen object directly. When using examples I get the behavior I want, but I just don't need animation or sequenced output on the terminal window.

Example:
```
# Doesn’t work, Color stays like the terminal emulator in configured
# This is in the style I would like to solve this problem, but I need some way to set the background of the complete screen
def demo(screen):
  while True:

    date = time.strftime("%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S")
    screen.print_at('Test menu', 0, 0)
    screen.print_at(date, screen.width-len(date), 0)
    screen.print_at('1) Some test menu entry', 3, 2)
    screen.print_at('2) Another test menu entry', 3, 3)
    # Look and feel
    # input
    # do something with select
    screen._change_colours(7, 0, 0)
    screen.refresh()

while True:
  try:
    Screen.wrapper(demo)
    sys.exit()
  except ResizeScreenError:
    pass

# Works, but I am not able to use print_at which is convenient, I also do not know why this makes the background black
# Also for some reason the images text is displayed in some kind of ticker way
# This feels too complicated for my use case
# Stolen from Christmas example, :-)
def demo(screen):
  while True:
    scenes = []
    date = time.strftime("%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S"),

    effects = [
    Print(screen, StaticRenderer(images=r'''This is a test'''),
      x=screen.width - 15,
      y=screen.height - 15,
      colour=Screen.COLOUR_GREEN),
    ]

    scenes.append(Scene(effects))

    screen.play(scenes)

while True:
  try:
    Screen.wrapper(demo)
    sys.exit()
  except ResizeScreenError:
    pass
```

In the last case, with the effect array, I also have problems just putting a string on the screen without resorting to some kind of predefined object. (Like the text box or regular box)

What I am looking for is being able to control the screen object in it's appearance, with the simplicity of the first example.

Thanks.

issue_title: Fill in event_at gaps with unseen values
issue_body:
Given:

```python
df = pd.DataFrame({"unit": [1, 2, 0, 2],
                   "place": [3, 1, 0, 2],
                   "tenure": [234, 352, 43, 821],
                   "terminated": [0, 1, 1, 0]})

cph = CoxPHFitter()
cph.fit(df, duration_col='tenure', event_col='terminated', show_progress=True, step_size=0.1)
```

I want to predict an event at a certain value, say 450. But I have not seen this event value.

```python
test_data = pd.DataFrame({"unit": [2, 3], 
                          "place": [2, 1]})
```

```
cph.predict_survival_function(test_data)

# returns
event_at	0	1
0.0	1.000000	1.000000
43.0	1.000000	0.999995
234.0	1.000000	0.999995
352.0	0.999983	0.516762
821.0	0.999983	0.516762
```

I know I can reach in at an event with the kmf like so:

```python
# Calculate the survival probability for t = 450
event_at_450 = kmf.event_table.loc[450, :]
surv_for_450 = (event_at_450.at_risk - event_at_450.observed) / event_at_450.at_risk
surv_for_450
```

But can I do this with a regressor and for unseen event_at?

issue_title: file upload input broken due to input width 100%
key hashing incorrectly uses 1 for empty strings
issue_body:
the [cmemcache_hash](https://github.com/linsomniac/python-memcached/blob/master/memcache.py#L68) function currently returns 1 for empty strings (or hash values which would result in 0).
Looking at the [libmemcache implementation](http://bazaar.launchpad.net/~tangent-trunk/libmemcached/1.0/view/head:/libhashkit/crc32.cc#L112), this selects a different server than libmemcache would. 
As far as i can see the correct version should remove the "or 1" from the code.

issue_title: TypeError: jwt_required() missing 1 required positional argument: 'fn'
Transition `on_execute` should accept the destination state as result even if there's only one destination.
issue_body:
* Python State Machine version: 0.5.0
* Python version: Any.
* Operating System: Any.

### Description

If you define a state machine with a transition that has an `on_execute` callback, you can't return the desired state if there's only one option. The syntax `return <result, desired state>` only works if multiple destination states are defined.

issue_title: meta.module_name should be meta.model_name for Django 1.8+

It should be meta.model_name

issue_title: Invalid path for a versioned K/V secrets engine
issue_body:
Server started with:
```
% vault server -dev
```
Example code from https://pypi.org/project/ansible-modules-hashivault/:
```
% export VAULT_ADDR='http://127.0.0.1:8200'
% cat >example.yml <<EOF
---
- hosts: localhost
  vars:
    foo_value: 'fum'
    fie_value: 'fum'
  tasks:
    - hashivault_status:
      register: 'vault_status'
    - hashivault_write:
        secret: 'giant'
        data:
            foo: '{{foo_value}}'
            fie: '{{fie_value}}'
      register: 'vault_write'
    - hashivault_read:
        secret: 'giant'
        key: 'fie'
      register: 'vault_read'

issue_title: [DISCUSS] API for improved image decoding/augmentation to keep up with future GPU architectures
issue_body:
Hi, this is Przemek from NVIDIA. While working on Volta support and optimizations for MXNet (and other frameworks) we stumbled upon a problem that I think requires discussion with the community. 

The benchmark that we use to assess CNN performance is ResNet 50 (v1). It turns out that with Volta, the GPU performance is high enough that we cannot feed the GPU with decoded and augmented images (even though the augmentations we use are actually pretty minimal - random resize, crop and mirror) fast enough (even though the testing system we use is very high end - 8 GPUs are paired with 40 CPU cores).

One could argue of course, that we should just move to a bigger model and circumvent this problem that way. One needs to also think about the inference time though - if the model becomes too big, then it becomes difficult to do inference e.g. on mobile device.

That is why we would like to tackle this problem somehow, and the most promising solutions require offloading at least some of the IO pipeline to the GPUs. However, this would be a pretty big change to MXNet's `fit` function, since right now the placement of IO pipeline is pretty rigid - the batch needs to be processed to be then split into parts for each GPU and sent to feed the actual training. The proposed approach would require iterator to split and send batches itself, which would require at least wrapping existing iterators.

What do you think about this issue and proposed solution? Any advice on how this could be implemented?

@tqchen @piiswrong @mli

issue_title: Implementing new operators in an external module
issue_body:
Currently it is possible to implement new operators in C++/CUDA, but it requires you to have your own copy of the source tree and build it into the standard mxnet library.

The only way to share such operators with others is to either share your fork of the MXNet repo or to contribute the operator back to the mainline MXNet project.

This is not scalable. It makes it very difficult to use operators from more than one fork, and it requires developers to build the entire source rather than just the source for their operators. It also requires developers of custom operators to manually merge from the parent MXNet project everytime they want to take advantage of the latest features and bug fixes unrelated to their own operators.

Ideally, there should be a way to define new operators in a separate project that is built against header files and shared library from a released version of MXNet, and then be able to load them from various MXNet front-end languages without having to replace all of MXNet with a custom version.

This may already be possible if you are coding directly in C++, but if you are using Python the mxnet shared library is installed in the Python library directory and is loaded in local mode so that other libraries cannot link against it.

Instead the mxnet shared libraries could be installed in a global location, independent of the front end being used, and loaded in global mode so that external modules could link against it. Each front-end language should provide a way to load an external module and verify that it is compatible with the currently installed copy of mxnet.

issue_title: How to communicate between apps pulsar, for example, through asyncio.Queue?
issue_body:
How to communicate between apps pulsar, for example, through asyncio.Queue?
The problem is that when the application starts somewhere inside the lost link to the asyncio.Queue object.
Prototype of uses:

```
import asyncio
import pulsar


class App1(pulsar.Application):
    queue = None

    async def watch_queue(self, worker):
        app = worker.app
        while 1:
            app.queue.put_nowait({'id': 1})  # Here app.queue is None

            await asyncio.sleep(1, loop=worker._loop)

    def worker_start(self, worker, exc=None):
        asyncio.ensure_future(self.watch_queue(worker), loop=worker._loop)


class App2(pulsar.Application):
    queue = None

    async def watch_queue(self, worker):
        app = worker.app
        app_queue = app.queue
        while 1:
            msg = app_queue.get()  # Here app.queue is None

            if not msg:
                await asyncio.sleep(1, loop=worker._loop)
                continue

            print(msg)

    def worker_start(self, worker, exc=None):
        asyncio.ensure_future(self.watch_queue(worker), loop=worker._loop)


class Server(pulsar.MultiApp):
    def build(self):
        App1.queue = asyncio.Queue()
        App2.queue = App1.queue

        yield self.new_app(App1)
        yield self.new_app(App2, prefix='app2')


async def run():
    server = Server()
    server.start()
```

Rerun task with same parameters without removing outputs
issue_body:
Hi folks,

```
 Recently, an requirement did trouble me a lot. I need to update the latest data file with `_latest.txt` suffix while I didn't want to generate the latest file with timestamp. So I couldn't rerun the task with same parameters. I have tried some methods on it, While It seems didn't work.

```
import luigi
import uuid

class FooTask(luigi.Task):

     foo = luigi.Parameter()

issue_title: PGPKey.parse is suddenly breaking when importing GPG keyrings with trust packets
issue_body:
It throws a UserWarning:

```python
pgpy/types.py:154: UserWarning: Warning: Orphaned packet detected! <Trust [tag 0x12] at 0x1040b1d30>
  po = obj.parse(data)
```

which is followed by a lovely stack trace:

```python
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-4-dc0db38409a2> in <module>()
----> 1 kr.load("ubuntu-archive-keyring.gpg")

PGPy/pgpy/pgp.py in load(self, *args)
   2147                         for item in ilist):
   2148             if os.path.isfile(key):
-> 2149                 _key, keys = PGPKey.from_file(key)
   2150 
   2151             else:

PGPy/pgpy/types.py in from_file(cls, filename)
    152             file.readinto(data)
    153 
--> 154         po = obj.parse(data)
    155 
    156         if po is not None:

PGPy/pgpy/pgp.py in parse(self, data)
   1977         while True:
   1978             # print(type(p) for p in getpkt)
-> 1979             for group in iter(group for _, group in itertools.groupby(getpkt, key=pktgrouper()) if not _.endswith('Opaque')):
   1980                 pkt = next(group)
   1981 

PGPy/pgpy/pgp.py in <genexpr>(.0)
   1977         while True:
   1978             # print(type(p) for p in getpkt)
-> 1979             for group in iter(group for _, group in itertools.groupby(getpkt, key=pktgrouper()) if not _.endswith('Opaque')):
   1980                 pkt = next(group)
   1981 

AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'endswith'
```

It looks like the issue is in `pktgrouper`:

```python
        def pktgrouper():
            class PktGrouper(object):
                def __init__(self):
                    self.last = None

                def __call__(self, pkt):
                    if pkt.header.tag != PacketTag.Signature:
                        self.last = '{:02X}_{:s}'.format(id(pkt), pkt.__class__.__name__)
                    return self.last
            return PktGrouper()
```

Because it's not taking the trust packets into account, it's grouping many of the signatures behind trust packets instead of behind keys and userids, which is not working.

As a test, I changed getpkt to pre-filter trust packets, and loading from the GPG keyring worked:

```python
getpkt = lambda d: Packet(d) if len(d) > 0 else None
# getpkt = iter(functools.partial(getpkt, data), None)
getpkt = filter(lambda p: p.header.tag != PacketTag.Trust, iter(functools.partial(getpkt, data), None))
```

I am not particularly happy with that solution, so I'm opening this issue to figure out a cleaner way of doing this. Maybe having pktgrouper also group Trust packets....

issue_title: S3 Object and ObjectVersion don't link together, ObjectVersion does not return ACLs.
issue_body:
This is two issues in particular that don't allow working with S3 Resource classes for Object and ObjectVersion and ACLs:

1. There's no way to get at the list of ObjectVersions for an Object. One has to do an inefficient Bucket.object_versions.all() to list all object versions.
I suggest adding a Object.versions.all() to list all versions of an object. This call can make a more efficient call to list_object_versions giving the prefix as the object key to limit the results to versions of this particular object.

2. Given an ObjectVersion, there's no way to get at the ACL for this version. One can only get the ACL for an Object. ACLs are version specific.
I suggest adding a ObjectVersion.Acl() sub-resource.

Related to this - creating an Object from an ObjectVersion somehow gets me to the latest version of an Object and not to the specific version. So, I can't simply create an Object from an ObjectVersion and try to get its Acl.

Please see sample code to replicate the issue:

```python
import sys
import boto3

s3 = boto3.resource('s3')

# change these
bucket_name = 'bucket'
key = 'key'
version = 'id'

bucket = s3.Bucket(bucket_name)
obj = bucket.Object(key)
    
# No way to get a list of versions of a particular s3.Object
# Suggest adding a sub-resource
# s3.Object.versions.all()

            
obj_version = s3.ObjectVersion(bucket_name, key, version)
print(obj_version.id)

# No way to directly get at the ACL specific to this version
# suggest adding a s3.ObjectVersion.Acl() sub-resource

# If I instead create an Object() from this ObjectVersion, I get a pointer to the latest Version, not the one I'm interested in.
obj = obj_version.Object()

print(obj.version_id)

assert(obj_version.id == obj.version_id)
```

issue_title: playing old replays
issue_body:
### SITUATION:
I wanted to extract some game information from replays from WCS Valencia 2018 using Blizzard's [API protocol](https://github.com/Blizzard/s2client-proto).  However, it appears an automatically downloaded Battle.net cache does not sufficiently enable a manual launch of the replay using an older version of the game client which allows protocol connections.

- The replay pack was downloaded and extracted from [the official WCS.battle.net site](https://wcs.starcraft2.com/en-us/news/21965429/Replay-Pack:-2018-WCS-Valencia/).
- These replays are versioned as 4.4.0.65895.  Using the battle.net-connected client that relaunches the replay offline, I can load and view these replays without issue.
- I can review other replays and extract its game data [using this protocol](https://github.com/Blizzard/s2client-proto/blob/master/docs/protocol.md) without issue (provided I already have the corresponding map version).
- I have tried on Windows 10 and Windows 7 machines; both produce this issue.  I haven't tried other OS.
- There doesn't appear to be any issue with the 65895 version, the API connection or socket communication in other replays or games.  Both matches and replays run normally with observation requests returning valid data.

### PROBLEM STATEMENT:

Cannot play older replays using an older version of the SC2 game client manually launched offline with a valid connection to a compatible API client.

### ERROR MESSAGE:

SC2APIProtocol.ResponseStartReplay.Error.LaunchError: 'The launch of this game makes reference to mod or map dependencies which are no longer available.'

### STEPS TO REPRODUCE:

1. remove existing Battle.net folder from the main SC2 application folder.
2. Modify the command-line launch with the `-PrePopulateCache` option per [these instructions](https://github.com/Blizzard/s2client-proto/issues/12).
  * Adding this option on the command line directly when launching a Base***** version also launches the downloader application, but it crashes.

issue_title: Suggestion: config writer objects as python context managers
issue_body:
This is just a suggestion for what I think would be a bit more pythonic implementation of the config writer objects that exist throughout gitpython. The basic pattern is:

- get a config writer
- set some values
- call `release()` on the config writer object

```
cw = origin.config_writer
cw.set("pushurl", "other_url")
cw.release()
```

This pattern could be replaced with a context manager by providing the special methods `__enter__()` and `__exit__()` on the object returned by the `config_writer` property. The `release()` call would then occur automatically when the context is left.
The following example would be equivalent to the above:

```
with origin.config_writer as cw:
    cw.pushurl = "other_url"
```
This change could probably be done in a backwards-compatible manner, so everyone would be happy and this great library would become a tiny bit more pythonic :)

class APIServer(object):

    @router.route('/testtest', methods=["GET"])
    def just_checking(self, request):
        raise Exception("nope")
```

from twisted.trial.unittest import SynchronousTestCase

class BugTest(SynchronousTestCase):

    def setUp(self):
        """
        Set up a L{StubTreq} pointed at the API server service.

        self.server = APIServer()
        self.resource = self.server.router.resource()
        self.treq = StubTreq(self.resource)

        response = self.successResultOf(self.treq.get(
            "http://localhost/testtest"))
        self.assertEqual(response.code, 200)

    def test_bug_should_pass(self):
        self.assertTrue(True)

issue_title: Basic schema migration implementation in PG storage backend
issue_body:
I propose that we store a ``schema_version`` (simple integer) in the ``metadata`` table.

Then on startup, instead of checking the existance of the ``records`` like we do currently, we compare the schema versions.

For each missing intermediary version, we run the script ``migration_N_N+1.sql``. Each migration script would update the ``schema_version`` value.

The ``schema.sql`` would always contain the last version of the schema and end up by setting the ``schema_version`` value.

issue_title: BibIndex: index datasets using parent's author
issue_body:
In the next days I will work on a task on BibIndex, I write here the proposal solution to have feedback about the future pull request that I will open.

We want to index the dataset records using the authors of a paper that is specified by id in the dataset record. For example, we want the search query `author:Ellis` to give both the papers and the datasets authored by Ellis. In the datasets the tag `786__w` contains the id of the papers using it (the "parent records").

A related discussion on Asana is https://app.asana.com/0/11661778102949/12201448138720

The proposal solution is to write a new `BibIndexParentAuthorTokenizer` extending `BibIndexMultiFieldTokenizer`. This new tokenizer will fetch phrases from the "parent record" and will return tokens using internally an instance of `BibIndexAuthorTokenizer`.

Since we want to use two tokenizers for the same field (`BibIndexParentAuthorTokenizer` and `BibIndexAuthorTokenizer` for the "author" field) we need to associate multiple indexes to the same field (but this is not supported by WebSearch) or we can use an "author" virtual index associated to two real indexes ("parentauthor" and "simpleauthor").

Since we also want to index the canonical author id in the "author" field, two more tokenizers are required (`BibIndexCanonicalAuthorTokenizer` and `BibIndexCanonicalAuthorParentTokenizer`). These two tokenizers will be associated to the virtual index "author".

At the moment the method `add_recID_range` of the class `WordTable` has an `if self.index_name in ('firstauthor', 'exactfirstauthor', ...): ...` that adds the canonical ids to the token list, but I think a tokenizer would be better.

For fetching the affected records in the indexing process the only solution we found is to extend the special case `if index_name in ('author', ...` in `get_recIDs_by_date_bibliographic()` (`bibindex_engine`).

All the above also for the "exactauthor" field.

issue_title: RFC Dropping INSPIRE-centric legacy code
issue_body:
Part of Invenio code is completely INSPIRE specific and went into the `v1.2.0` (future former `master` branch) partially because of the limitations in plugging optional functionalities in Invenio. Nowadays thanks to the modular approach of `v2.0.0` it is possible to separate such code and move it where it is due to, i.e. the INSPIRE overlay.

This part amount to:
- [ ] pdfchecker (a completely INSPIRE-specific module, needed to refresh a given HEP record related to an arXiv record, upon arXiv updates)
- [ ] HEPData (this functionality is scattered around in miscutils and search engine, in particular because it introduces a new *Data* tab in the detailed record view.

issue_title: docs: document configuration
docs: document configuration
Ability to set Corporate proxy settings [Feature Request]
issue_body:
Corporate proxy settings can be set with the below bash script:

#!/bin/bash
export REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE=/home/$USER/cacert.pem
export http_proxy=http://***.***.***.**:8080
export https_proxy=https://***.***.***.**:8080
echo "Shell setup for Corporate Proxy" 

The problem is jarsigner does not use these environment variables. See below:

```
Signing the new APK may have failed.
jarsigner: unable to sign jar: no response from the Timestamping Authority. When connecting from behind a firewall an HTTP or HTTPS proxy may need to be specified. Supply the following options to jarsigner:
  -J-Dhttp.proxyHost=<hostname>
  -J-Dhttp.proxyPort=<portnumber>
or
  -J-Dhttps.proxyHost=<hostname> 
  -J-Dhttps.proxyPort=<portnumber> 


Signed the new APK
```

-J-Dhttps.proxyHost=***.***.***.** -J-Dhttps.proxyPort=8080 can't be passed to Objection as an option and as far as I can see from reading online there is not a config file where these settings can be added to for jarsigner.

issue_title: Distance calculation takes (y, x) instead of (x, y)
issue_body:
So the issue is that distance caluclation (`vincenty` and `great_circle`) take arguments in the form of 
 `((y, x), (y, x))` instead of `((x, y), (x, y))`. The convention followed by Geopy is consistent with the idea of `(Latitude, Longitude)`, but is not conversant with Shapely or Fiona's idea of the same. Shapely and Fiona will return `(x, y)`, or `(Longitude, Latitude)`, and I think Geopy should accept that format as default. Perhaps have a Boolean switch that takes the other format. For example, the following pseudocode:

```
def vincenty(p1, p2, xy=True):
    if xy:
        p1 = reversed(p1)
        p2 = reversed(p2)
    # And the rest of the algorithm as usual.
```

IMHO, this would save a considerable amount of headache. Most answers on Stack Exchange are also oblivious to this (eg: http://gis.stackexchange.com/questions/4022/looking-for-a-pythonic-way-to-calculate-the-length-of-a-wkt-linestring).

issue_title: Add long_short flag for performance.alpha_beta and modify tearsheet
issue_body:
I recommend adding a long_short flag to the **performance.factor_alpha_beta()** function along with associated functionality in the function code. My rationale is that when running **tears.create_full_tear_sheet()** with the long_short flag set to False, the "Returns Analysis" table still shows the alpha and more importantly the beta being calculated by regressing the factor-weighted long-short portfolio vs. the universe mean return, as opposed to the factor-weighted long-only portfolio. vs the universe mean return. 

I can work on this and submit a pull request, but I would appreciate it if someone could look over my rationale and comment as to whether you think this makes sense or not before I make the changes.

So seeing this in the official website would be a huge bonus to me, and I guess, for many others who sometimes spend minutes looking through all the 600+ icons just to find the one we are looking for.

issue_title: Incorrect validation of required body parameters.
issue_body:
### Description

Here is the OpenAPI specification which causes problems:

```yaml
swagger: '2.0'

consumes:
  - application/json
produces:
  - application/json

paths:
  /token:
    post:
      summary: Send tokens from the current user to another user
      parameters:
        - name: payload
          in: body
          required: true
          schema:
            properties:
              value:
                type: integer
            required:
              - value
      responses:
        200:
          description: Success
```

__main__.py:
```python
from pkg_resources import resource_filename
import connexion
from connexion.resolver import RestyResolver

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app = connexion.App(__name__, specification_dir='.')
    app.add_api(resource_filename(__name__, 'openapi.yml'), resolver=RestyResolver('rest_api_bug'))
    app.run(port=8080, host='0.0.0.0')
```

endpoint.py:
```python
from connexion import NoContent


def post(payload):
    print(payload)
    return NoContent, 200
```
### Expected behaviour

When there is no body in a POST request one should get a 400 error.

### Actual behaviour

`None` value is being passed to the request handler. On the other hands, it handles malformed JSON. For example, if one will pass just `{}` in the request body, a 400 error will be thrown.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Start the API server.
2. Send POST request with no body: `curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/endpoint`
3. No error is thrown, `payload` in `post` is `None`.

### Additional info:

- Python 3.6.5
- Connexion 1.4

issue_title: Properly cancelling task during pending MQTT subscription
issue_body:
I am trying to create a task, which consumes data from MQTT topics and does some processing. As this is long term running process, it runs in `while True` loop and I want to cancel the task properly by sending `task.cancel()`.

However, the `cancel()` results in exceptions thrown from within `RuntimeError: 'Event loop is closed.')"`

Here is the code which is adapted from project examples:


	import logging
	import asyncio

	from hbmqtt.client import MQTTClient, ClientException
	from hbmqtt.mqtt.constants import QOS_1, QOS_2

	logger = logging.getLogger(__file__)
	logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)


	async def reader_coro(mqtt_url, loop):

		while True:

issue_title: Lister les variables calculables en fonction des données saisies
issue_body:
Est-il possible de lister les variables calculables par rapport aux données disponibles ?

Par exemple, pour calculer le montant d'aide au logement (APL), il faut saisir des informations relatives au logement, à la composition familiale, la situation professionnelle, et aux revenus. (source: [site de la caf](https://wwwd.caf.fr/wps/portal/caffr/aidesetservices/lesservicesenligne/estimervosdroits/lelogement/!ut/p/a1/lZDNDoIwEISfxQcwnVp_4NiAQKkK_qDCxTRGTaNUg8bntyQeDIkY97bJN7M7QwqyJYVRT31SD3016lLvxXCXJjSM5BIhxj0HIkLKomjNkPQskFsAX4ajVe_grW8BftzfkKKB-KkP4SKWrjugoaRNwAm8wAID35_Nhf2R_QLwBlpC5jbF6MNhljDwMKWOjD0K9ElmrlVp-1zWXseqPHtGHfm-bpnk1UEb_dDqou-HqumVUO5CLCYyDoYMNt8_Xrcy20KLbl7upive6bwA5ZjMGA!!/dl5/d5/L3dDcEEvd0NvQk1rQkEvNE8weENPd2cvWjZfUE8xR0hLUzBHMEUyODBJSDBQM0hIVjMwTzIvWjdfUE8xR0hLUzBHTzFBOTBJUkxLSkY2MzAwRzA!/))

Il serait donc pratique d'avoir comme sortie "aide_logement" lorsque je précise que je détient ces informations.

Le comportement actuel semble être d'utiliser des variables par défaut. 
Par exemple, si je demande de calculer le RSA sans préciser de salaire, alors j'ai bien un RSA qui est calculé (~500€ par mois, ce qui doit correspondre à un salaire de zéro). 
Dans le cas du RSA, si l'utilisateur ne m'a pas encore fournis son salaire, je voudrais savoir qu'il ne sera pas pertinent de lui afficher son aide RSA.

Je pourrais implémenter de mon côté une sorte de mapping entre les données disponibles et les aides mais dans ce cas on a un problème de responsabilité des systèmes car openfisca ne serait plus le seul "sachant" concernant la manière de calculer les différentes aides.

issue_title: Symbolic exploration sometimes leads to incorrect results
issue_body:
### OS / Environment

Distributor ID:	ArchLinux
Description:	ArchLinux
Release:	rolling-release

### Manticore version

Last revision (465c926a2e39d5daf1405711dac61b6ddd0a2527)

### Python version

Python 2.7.13

### Summary of the problem

Using symbolic exploration leads to incorrect results. This means that sometimes a function returns an unexpected value.

### Step to reproduce the behavior

This is a python script to reproduce this issue:

```
from os.path import isdir
from manticore.ethereum import ManticoreEVM

cpus = 10
workspace = "ecrecover_out"
assert not isdir(workspace)
m = ManticoreEVM(procs=cpus, workspace_url=workspace)

m.verbosity(3)
source_code = """
contract C {

    function reveal(uint x, bytes32 hash) returns (bool) {
        if (sha3(x) == hash) {
            return true;
        }

        return false;
    }
}
"""

owner_account = m.create_account(balance=10000000)
print "[+] Creating a owner account", hex(owner_account)

print "[+] Creating a contract account",
contract_account = m.solidity_create_contract(source_code, owner=owner_account)

symbolic_data = m.make_symbolic_buffer(320)
symbolic_value = m.make_symbolic_value()
m.transaction(caller=owner_account,
              address=contract_account,
              data=symbolic_data,
              value=symbolic_value )

m.finalize()
```
Just run this script and then use grep to look for the return values of reveal:

```
$ grep "reveal" ecrecover_out/*.tx -A 1                                                             
```

this results in two test contradictory test cases:

```
...
ecrecover_out/test_00000004.tx:reveal(64934387297789082139492748455852434600069050224261179049578174561300304072591L,'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00') -> RETURN (*)
ecrecover_out/test_00000004.tx-return: 1 
--
ecrecover_out/test_00000005.tx:reveal(64934387297789082139492748455852434600069050224261179049578174561300304072591L,'\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01') -> RETURN (*)
ecrecover_out/test_00000005.tx-return: 0
```

It is interesting to note that if we replace the hash in the if condition by any fixed value (e.g 0x1), manticore will find only the correct test case (return: 0). 

### Expected behavior

I expect manticore to show only the correct test case:

```
ecrecover_out/test_00000005.tx:reveal(64934387297789082139492748455852434600069050224261179049578174561300304072591L,'\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01\x01') -> RETURN (*)
ecrecover_out/test_00000005.tx-return: 0
```

issue_title: Mark-to-base positioning rule error
makeotf always exits with 0 (i.e. success)  even when error occurs
issue_body:
The MakeOTF.py script's main() function _does not_ return a non-zero value to the calling shell when an exception occurs and the font was not built successfully. Currently _all_ exceptions are handled and passed, so main always exits successfully:

https://github.com/adobe-type-tools/afdko/blob/56befefb4b3f6daa26d670d05e3c95df4ec32dd5/afdko/Tools/SharedData/FDKScripts/MakeOTF.py#L3063-L3073

in fontmake there's an optional FDKFeatureCompiler that calls makeotf as subprocess. That code is broken for many reasons, one of which is that it attempts to guess whether the build was successful from the messages printed to stdout, because the exit code is meaningless.

issue_title: Protobufs Dependencies Prevents Vendoring gcloud-python on GAE
issue_body:
GAE requires that you vendor all libraries with a sandbox, pip install -r requirements.txt -t lib/ is the current recommended approach. When you do this with gcloud-python as a dependency, it includes a package named google which contains the protobuf dependency. The problem is that you _also_ need to have the AppEngine SDK on your path which is also in a package named google, so if you put GAE first on your path, gcloud-python will fail to import protobufs, and if you put lib/ first, you can't import appengine SDK libs.

I think @jonparrott might have some more thoughts on how to resolve this (namespaced packages?)

issue_title: Can not reopen Pub/Sub subscription with a different callback
issue_body:
I'm trying to do a simple thing: open Pub/Sub subscription with one callback just to drain out all messages that are currently stored in the subscription. Then reopen the same subscription with a different callback to do a proper processing. Unfortunately that doesn't seem to work.

The first time I open subscription like this:
```python
    drained_num = [0]

    def discard_callback(message):
        drained_num[0] += 1
        message.ack()

    future = subscription.open(discard_callback)

    try:
        future.result(timeout=2)
    except TimeoutError:
        pass
    except Exception as ex:
        logger.error("Caught unexpected exception!", exc_info=True)
    finally:
        logger.info("Discarded %d messages", drained_num[0])
        subscription.close()
```

After that I try to reopen subscription with a different callback:
```python
    def callback(message):
        pass

    future = subscription.open(callback)

    try:
        future.result(timeout=3)
    except TimeoutError:
        logger.info("Time is up!")
    except Exception as ex:
        logger.error("Caught unexpected exception!", exc_info=True)
    finally:
        logger.info("Closing connection")
        subscription.close()
```

And this fails with the following error:
```
MainThread:139861846668480 [ERROR] Caught unexpected exception!
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "scripts/publish-receive.py", line 85, in main
    future.result(timeout=args.timeout)
  File "PATH/venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/pubsub_v1/futures.py", line 103, in result
    raise err
RuntimeError: cannot schedule new futures after shutdown
```

I tried to don't call `subscription.close()` after reading subscription the first time but then I get another error:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "scripts/publish-receive.py", line 98, in <module>
    main()
  File "scripts/publish-receive.py", line 81, in main
    future = subscription.open(callback)
  File "PATH/venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/pubsub_v1/subscriber/policy/thread.py", line 261, in open
    raise ValueError('This policy has already been opened.')
ValueError: This policy has already been opened.
```

So, my question is it even possible to reopen subscription with a different callback?
I'm using `google-cloud-pubsub-0.30.1`.

issue_title: serving from a folder instead of a .model file
issue_body:
Why can't I serve from a folder?
I think that it makes sense to have this option. The main reason is that if you're making a new model, you're constantly fiddling with things. If you have to export it every single update, it is a huge waste of effort, and you end up making a new folder every time the server extracts the model archive. 

You should be able to drop the `.model` and just point to a folder like:

```
mxnet-model-server --models squeezenet=squeezenet_v1.1
```

Also, think about this: you first download one via s3, then serve it, then go in and tinker with the settings. Then you export it and it overwrites the original model. Oops. Then you serve it and it overwrites the directory with the original files. Oops. It doesn't work (because reasons), so you pull the original down and serve it, and that overwrites your changes in the folder. Oops again.

issue_title: Support environment variables without values
issue_body:
See https://github.com/docker/compose/pull/2997 for some context.

docker-py assumes that all variables should have an `=` but the docker-cli passes environment variables without values as just the key, which is super weird, but apparently swarm is relying on that currently.

issue_title: Small issue with pypandoc and common_setup (hit while installing pytest-profiling)
issue_body:
If the `pypandoc` module is installed but `pandoc` is not then `import pypandoc` will succeed but the `pypandoc` module will not have a `convert` attribute when imported, leading to an `AttributeError` being raised, which common_setup does not currently catch.

The workaround for us is to install `pandoc` before doing our `pip` installs so that the `convert` attribute exists when it needs to.

Due to the [Security considerations](http://docs.deis.io/en/latest/managing_deis/security_considerations/)  in our CoreOS cluster I configured etcd to [use SSL and client certificates](https://coreos.com/docs/distributed-configuration/etcd-security/), it works fine, but I cannot make deisctl to start platform.

Configuring, listing, installing worked, but starting did not.

I have checked logs and the Unit Files (eg: `deis-store-monitor.service`), and I think it hangs at `ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c "etcdctl set /deis/store/hosts/$COREOS_PRIVATE_IPV4 `hostname` >/dev/null"` because `etcdctl` cannot access etcd, and I have no idea how to tell etcdctl to use client certificate. 

I would appreciate any idea, thanks.

issue_title: Tighten linting rules to catch white space inconsistencies
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Our current linting rules don't catch certain inconsistencies around the handling of white space.
Notice the mix of white space / no white space around the `=` symbol in the following code.

```python

    def __init__(
        self,
        number: int,
        transaction_root: bytes = EMPTY_SHA3,
        coinbase: bytes=ZERO_ADDRESS,
          ) -> None:
        ...
```

Our current `flake8` linting doesn't catch this. The official style guide [favors to use the spaces around the `=` symbol](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/#variable-annotations) in this case which is enforceable using using `pylint`.


### How can it be fixed

- switch to black formatter (See discussion #476)
- add a `pylint` check
- write a flake8 plugin (maybe?)

issue_title: Need timeouts and proper task-cancellation when dealing with trinity's proxied-services
issue_body:
Currently our chain syncer implementation do things like:

```
await wait_with_token(chaindb.coro_import_block(block), token=self.cancel_token)
```

Where chaindb is a `multiprocessing` proxy that runs the actual block importing in a separate process. Since we're dealing with a separate process that may die halfway through importing the block, or hang completely and never respond, I believe we should always specify timeouts when dealing with them -- in the same way we do for network operations.

Also, by wrapping those with wait_with_token() only cancels the outer task and not the one that is actually doing the work, which is created inside coro_import_block() by the await loop.run_in_executor(...) call. Maybe we just need to pass the CancelToken to coro_import_block() and have it use that with wait_with_token() instead of us.

issue_title: Remove non-standard tag from wgn filename
issue_body:
Currently, Wagon appends two additional fields to each archive's file name if running on Linux - distribution name and distribution codename. These tags are not a part of the wheel standard and are only there to help with installing on Linux (if not all wheels are manylinux1). 

To accommodate the need to still identify the Linux distribution and release the wagon was created on, these tags should be removed from the file name and remain in the metadata only (where they also currently reside). Once manylinux1 is (hopefully) easier to generate, we would be able to deprecate this information from the metadata as well.

Practically, this means that if you want to auto-identify on which distribution and release you most probably can install the wagon, you would have to either use the CLI or the API to retrieve its metadata and read the relevant fields.

issue_body:
```
import elasticsearch
import curator
client = elasticsearch.Elasticsearch('https://somecoolcluster')
ilo = curator.IndexList(client)
print("Indices: {}".format(ilo.indices))
```

```
$ pip list
click (6.6)
elasticsearch (2.3.0)
elasticsearch-curator (4.0.4)
pip (8.1.2)
PyYAML (3.11)
setuptools (20.10.1)
urllib3 (1.16)
```

```
GET /_cluster/state/metadata/someindex [status:401 request:0.004s]
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./es_curation.py", line 43, in <module>
    ilo = curator.IndexList(client)
  File "/home/ubuntu/.pyenv/versions/3.5.2/lib/python3.5/site-packages/curator/indexlist.py", line 30, in __init__
    self.__get_indices()
  File "/home/ubuntu/.pyenv/versions/3.5.2/lib/python3.5/site-packages/curator/indexlist.py", line 70, in __get_indices
    self._get_metadata()
  File "/home/ubuntu/.pyenv/versions/3.5.2/lib/python3.5/site-packages/curator/indexlist.py", line 147, in _get_metadata
    index=to_csv(l),metric='metadata'
  File "/home/ubuntu/.pyenv/versions/3.5.2/lib/python3.5/site-packages/elasticsearch/client/utils.py", line 69, in _wrapped
    return func(*args, params=params, **kwargs)
  File "/home/ubuntu/.pyenv/versions/3.5.2/lib/python3.5/site-packages/elasticsearch/client/cluster.py", line 75, in state
    'state', metric, index), params=params)
  File "/home/ubuntu/.pyenv/versions/3.5.2/lib/python3.5/site-packages/elasticsearch/transport.py", line 329, in perform_request
    status, headers, data = connection.perform_request(method, url, params, body, ignore=ignore, timeout=timeout)
  File "/home/ubuntu/.pyenv/versions/3.5.2/lib/python3.5/site-packages/elasticsearch/connection/http_urllib3.py", line 109, in perform_request
    self._raise_error(response.status, raw_data)
  File "/home/ubuntu/.pyenv/versions/3.5.2/lib/python3.5/site-packages/elasticsearch/connection/base.py", line 108, in _raise_error
    raise HTTP_EXCEPTIONS.get(status_code, TransportError)(status_code, error_message, additional_info)
elasticsearch.exceptions.AuthenticationException: TransportError(401, '{"Message":"Your request: \'/_cluster/state/metadata/someindex\' is not allowed."}')
```

AWS ES cluster with ES version 2.3. It appears getting a list of the indexes includes calls to /_cluster/state/ which isn't allowed by AWS ([reference](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticsearch-service/latest/developerguide/es-gsg-supported-operations.html)).

It seems that the metadata call currently isn't wrapped up in a way that would allow it to fail gracefully ([reference](https://github.com/elastic/curator/blob/master/curator/indexlist.py#L70)).

Is it reasonable to return the index list with less data in the cases where we get back TransportError with status 401 for the metadata call? As it is now Curator is not compatible with AWS Elasticsearch Service with ES 2.3.

issue_title: Exclude should only be True when explicitely set to True
issue_body:
In a curator action file you can set `exclude: True` on a filter to negate its effect. As setting this accidentally can destroy much data, this setting should only accept "True" or "False" or empty (meaning the default: False). However, adding a yaml list below exclude is also interpreted as True.

Here is an example:

Intended configuration that works as expected, removing all indices starting with test, older than 14 days:

```yaml
actions:
  1:
    action: delete_indices
    description: >-
      Delete indices older than 14 days
    options:
      ignore_empty_list: True
      timeout_override:
      continue_if_exception: False
      disable_action: False
    filters:
      - filtertype: pattern
        kind: prefix
        value: test
        exclude:
      - filtertype: age
        source: name
        direction: older
        timestring: '%Y.%m.%d'
        unit: days
        unit_count: 14
```

Accidentally wrongly indented configuration that deletes all indices except those starting with test:

```yaml
actions:
  1:
    action: delete_indices
    description: >-
      Delete indices older than 14 days
    options:
      ignore_empty_list: True
      timeout_override:
      continue_if_exception: False
      disable_action: False
    filters:
      - filtertype: pattern
        kind: prefix
        value: test
        exclude:
        - filtertype: age
          source: name
          direction: older
          timestring: '%Y.%m.%d'
          unit: days
          unit_count: 14
```

Tested on:
Curator Version: 4.2.6
Elasticsearch Version: 5.2.2

issue_title: azure login through interactive mode: cant recognizer user identifier or password
issue_body:
### Description
Basically, I already solved my problem, and as I did not find any information to such kind of issues, in case if somebody else will face that too, I decided to describe it here. 

So, I was having an issue during interactive login using Azure CLI 1.0 from my Linux VM. And the key of interactive login is that you need to follow the link and input the code from the command line. As I was connected to my VM only through Putty from Windows, I was not be able to go to that link and specify the code, thats whyI was using my browser on Windows. And the thing is when you trying to do that, it shows an error in the browser like this: 

> Can not recognizer user identifier or password

And it solves just installing the CLI tool on Windows and following all the steps for login. 
Hope somebody will find this helpful.

P.S.: please fix problems with urllib3 on CLI 2.0 login 
P.S.S.: put it to the appropriate place as FAQ, whatever. Or delete it if there are already such info provided.

issue_title: [Compute] Availability Set create defaults can be invalid
issue_body:
The following defaults:
```
--platform-fault-domain-count : Default: 5.
--platform-update-domain-count: Default: 5.
```
can be invalid depending on the region the availability set is created in. For example, when I tried to create a VM in westus with an availability set in westus, I got a deployment error requesting I use a value between 1 and 3.

We should either set the default to something that works across all regions or see if there's a way to query what the default should be for the selected region.

Having a lot of trouble running Azure cli commands so had to do some research.
In order to deploy to Azure via the azure-cli, apparently its best to use an updated production python version which in this case is python3. So we want to install python3 and pip3 but need to do it into a different directory because this mac uses python2.7 internally and we dont want to mess anything up. See <http://docs.python-guide.org/en/latest/starting/install/osx/> and this SO post:
<http://stackoverflow.com/questions/33535625/python-3-5-for-os-x-el-capitan>.

Going to have to figure out how we specify which version to use.

## Installing python3 and pip3

I went to the [python docs](http://docs.python-guide.org/en/latest/starting/install/osx/) to learn how to install python3. Run `brew install python3` in terminal. After this installs, lets check if pip3 is installed or we have to do something else. Got this output:

Running `pip3 install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel` per instructions though cant say im sure what this does. Lots of this going on in dev work. ```bash

Python 2.7.10
klik:~ klik$ which python
/usr/bin/python

Python 3.6.0
klik:~ klik$ pip --version
pip 9.0.1 from /Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/pip-9.0.1-py2.7.egg (python 2.7)

pip 9.0.1 from /usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages (python 3.6)
klik:~ klik$ 
```

So now I have the recommended python3/pip3 for azure cli. Lets see if azure cli is working now....not working getting the same errors.

## Retrying Azure-cli

I lost focus for a second and ran `azure -help` and `az -help`. The first one ran but not the second. I then tried the alternative solution in azure-cli troubleshooting on [issue on github](https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/1308#issuecomment-260413613) and ran `pip install --pre azure-cli`. This gave me the following error after a bunch of terminal output: 

```bash
...
Permission denied: '/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/azure_cli-0.1.0b11-py3.5-nspkg.pth'

I then tried to run the same command with sudo: `sudo ` and got an error message suggesting I use the python -H flag which, per this stackoverflow post <http://stackoverflow.com/questions/27870003/pip-install-please-check-the-permissions-and-owner-of-that-directory>, sets the $HOME variable to the target user. Later in that post, its suggested run the command with the --user flag and I did this instead just because it seemed cleaner. So I ran `pip install --user --pre azure-cli`. So this is almost the same command as the original install instructions except we added the -pre flag. I figured that I was back at the original install instructions workflow and tried running the next step `az component update --add appservice`. This threw the following error in the terminal:

```bash
