issue_title: az provider register should provide wait / no-wait
issue_body:
```bash
$ az provider register --namespace "Microsoft.Storage"
Registering is still on-going. You can monitor using 'az provider show -n Microsoft.Storage'
null
```

"You can monitor using `az provider show...`" doesn't seem like a reasonable way to handle a request for registration. If the rest of the script requires that resource provider to be registered, the consumer of this command will need to loop poling `az provider show` to determine the request result.

There should be wait / no-wait on this command. Wait should wait for the registration to complete and return either an error or the body of the provider.

issue_title: [Network] ExpressRoute inadequate server-side validation
issue_body:
The following requirements are not validated by the ExpressRoute service, resulting in confusing errors and/or unexpected behavior:

1. Attempting to create MicrosoftPeering settings on an ExpressRoute circuit with a "Standard" SKU type yields a vague error ("An error occured.") and puts the circuit into a corrupt state. The service should validate that the SKU is compatible with the peering type and throw an actionable error.

2. Attempting to create MicrosoftPeering settings with a VLAN id that is already in use results in apparent success, but the peering relationship does not actually appear (for example, in the portal). The service should validate that the VLAN id is not in use or throw an actionable error.

issue_title: Implement `pre-commit exec` or similar?
issue_body:
Hi,

I regularly find myself in the use case that I want to troubleshoot or run a specific command for one of pre commit hook. A typical use-case would be running `rubocop --auto-gen-config` after upgrading version. The first times I've tried just editing my `.pre-commit-config.yaml` to add this argument, but for this command it doesn't work with the batching pre-commit does because of command length limits, since rubocop expects to scan all the repo at once to know all wrong offenses and generate the file.

Right now I'm manually finding the local repo with something like `find ~/.cache/pre-commit/ -iname "rubocop*.gemspec"` and then having to manually `. rbenv-default/bin/activate` from there, exporting a few environment variables, and finally being able to run my commands with the virtual env all setup.

This is a bit tedious, and I was wondering if `pre-commit exec rubocop <args>` (like `docker exec ...`) would be a suitable PR idea. Happy to work on that

issue_body:
I having issue porting my v1 project to v2 project. If I instanciate an object using 

```
model = MyModel()
```

rather than 

```
model = MyModel({})
```

I end up using defaults as reference and not copy. Ending with all subsequent objects to inherit their sibling value as default.

```
import unittest

from schematics import Model
from schematics.types import ListType, StringType

class MyModel(Model):
    items = ListType(StringType, default=[])


class TestIterable(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_without_value(self):
        model = MyModel()
        model.items.append('foo')
        model.items.append('bar')

        self.assertEqual(len(model.items), 2)

        model2 = MyModel()
        self.assertEqual(len(model2.items), 0)  # This won't work. DUH. 

    def test_with_value(self):
        model = MyModel({})
        model.items.append('foo')
        model.items.append('bar')

        self.assertEqual(len(model.items), 2)

        model2 = MyModel({})

issue_title: stop relying on git to handle absolute paths in imports
issue_body: Right now absolute paths in imports are still passed to git, which relativizes them. That's a security vulnerability -- the git defaults might change, or we might switch implementations without remembering this detail. We should stop doing it, and also create a test for it.

issue_title: It should be a warning to override a module that doesn't exist.
"read_trip_ids_by_day" - custom service day hours
issue_body:
### Feature request

Adding an option to get "trip id by day", considering a custom "service day" hours (not necessarily midnight to midnight).

### Description 

"Service day" definition may vary between applications.  
For example, it is more convenient to define a service day of 4AM to 4AM of the next date, in order to analyze GTFS for working days.   
To answer the question: "When is the earliest departure of route x at date y" it might be irrelevant that there is a departure of route x on 00:30 AM of date y.

### Suggestion

An option for implementation is to filter trip_ids by something similar to the following method, copied from Paul Harrington's post on https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/transit-developers/ZkfnuNv1gho : 
"When searching for next departures at a stop at a time between midnight and 3am I rolled back a day and added 24 hours to the hour so instead of running a query for departures at a stop starting from a  date of 20180124 and a time of 01:00:00 I would use 20180123 and 25:00:00"

The usage could be something like adding "read_trip_ids_by_day" method, which is similar to "read_service_ids_by_date", but also gets "day_start" and "day_end" hours as inputs. 
Then we would be able to filter the "feed" by these trip ids.
```
import datetime
import partridge as ptg

path = 'path/to/sfmta-2017-08-22.zip'

trip_ids_by_day = ptg.read_trip_ids_by_day(path, day_start="03:00:00", day_end="02:59:59")

trip_ids = trip_ids_by_day[datetime.date(2017, 9, 25)]
# Now "trip_ids" should contain all the trip_ids of trips that start 
#  between 25-09-2017 03:00:00 and 26-09-2017 02:59:59

feed = ptg.feed(path, view={
    'trips.txt': {
        'trip_id': trip_ids,
    },
})
```

### Thanks!!

issue_title: jwk thumbprint is mismade
issue_body:
The JWK.thumbprint method isn't quite right. Going off the go-jose test vectors, this RSA public key JWK (with whitespace removed): 

    {
        "kty": "RSA",
        "kid": "bilbo.baggins@hobbiton.example",
        "use": "sig",
        "n": "n4EPtAOCc9AlkeQHPzHStgAbgs7bTZLwUBZdR8_KuKPEHLd4rHVTeT-O-XV2jRojdNhxJWTDvNd7nqQ0VEiZQHz_AJmSCpMaJMRBSFKrKb2wqVwGU_NsYOYL-QtiWN2lbzcEe6XC0dApr5ydQLrHqkHHig3RBordaZ6Aj-oBHqFEHYpPe7Tpe-OfVfHd1E6cS6M1FZcD1NNLYD5lFHpPI9bTwJlsde3uhGqC0ZCuEHg8lhzwOHrtIQbS0FVbb9k3-tVTU4fg_3L_vniUFAKwuCLqKnS2BYwdq_mzSnbLY7h_qixoR7jig3__kRhuaxwUkRz5iaiQkqgc5gHdrNP5zw",
     "e": "AQAB"
    }

must be thumprinted into `f63838e96077ad1fc01c3f8405774dedc0641f558ebb4b40dccf5f9b6d66a932`.

I think the problem is that the `json.dumps` the thumbprint method uses can mis-order the keys in the JSON hash. There is an order that [RFC7638](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7638#section-3.3) that the keys must be in. The go-jose code does that with this line of code: https://github.com/square/go-jose/blob/4ddd71883fa547d37fbf598071f04512d8bafee3/jwk.go#L155

issue_title: Python vs curl response times
issue_body:
I am seeing very slow response times on API requests to _gateway.watsonplatform.net_ via Python.

As an example:

- A [Get information about a Classifier](https://www.ibm.com/watson/developercloud/natural-language-classifier/api/v1/curl.html?curl#get-information-about-a-classifier) request to Watson NLC with Python takes **75-150 seconds** to respond.

- The same request with Curl responds instantaneously.

- The problem is also evident when calling other services; e.g., Watson NLU.

- The problem is evident only when calling the endpoint _https://gateway.watsonplatform.net/natural-language-classifier/api_ .   The problem is _not_ evident when calling the API Explorer _https://watson-api-explorer.mybluemix.net/apis/natural-language-classifier-v1_ .

- This is a recent development, first noticed about one week ago.

Please see the attached scripts for examples.

The only combination of factors that reproducibly returns a slow response is a Python call to the default endpoint _gateway.watsonplatform.net_ .

Are there any known issues, or recent changes to the environment that might cause the slow response?

#### python sdk version
1.2.1

#### python version
Issue occurs with both Python 2.7.10 and Python 3.6.1

#### OS
Mac OS High Sierra 10.13.3

#### IBM Cloud instance
US South

[nlcClassifierStatusSamplePython.txt](https://github.com/watson-developer-cloud/python-sdk/files/1853470/nlcClassifierStatusSamplePython.txt)

issue_title: Create dummy class for Facebook API tests
issue_body:
In order to test the portions of our application that use the Facebook API, it looks like the best approach is to avoid making actual API calls and instead return hard-coded copies of the responses. The rationale behind this is that our unit tests are really only meant to test *our* code, and therefor we should simply assume that external integrations are working properly.

Here's what I'm thinking:

Add an optional argument [here](https://github.com/ubyssey/dispatch/blob/Event-forms-etc/dispatch/apps/events/facebook.py#L10-L13) that allows you to specify a custom `Facebook` API class. The default value can just be the normal [Facebook](https://github.com/ubyssey/dispatch/blob/Event-forms-etc/dispatch/vendor/apis.py#L6) class, but for our tests we'll pass in `FacebookTest` instead.

`FacebookTest` will define the same methods as `Facebook` (e.g. `get_event`), but will simply return JSON directly instead of making an API call. For example, the `get_event` method might look like this:

```py

# constant that holds dummy event data
EVENTS = {
    17: {
        'title': 'this is a title',
        'description': 'this is a description',
        ...
    }
}

fb.get_event(17) # returns event 17 from above

issue_title: dump_only is ignored in nested schemas for parameters
Field-level validation errors cannot be saved on a nested field with many=True
issue_body:
When validating a field, all of its error messages are saved and appended to the list of messages for that field name in `call_and_store`, but for `fields.Nested` with `many=True` the error messages for child elements of the collection are saved and stored in a dictionary keyed by the element index. This causes the following exception on this minimal test case:
```python
class Inner(Schema):
    req = fields.Field(required=True)

class Outer(Schema):
    inner = fields.Nested(Inner, many=True)

    @validates('inner')
    def validates_inner(self, data):
        raise ValidationError('not a chance')

outer = Outer()
_, errors = outer.load({'inner': [{}]})
```

issue_title: Wrong answers and segmentation faults with try-for and qe-sat tactic
issue_body:
This issue is actually an adaptation of a stackoverflow question (http://stackoverflow.com/questions/31864004/z3-try-for-causes-segmentation-faults) so I will copy some parts of this question.

I am using the Java-API to communicate with Z3 on Ubuntu Linux 14.04 for the development of a Scala application. Regarding Z3, I use the current version of the master-branch found on github (compiled on the 6th of August).

The basic structure of the formulas, for which I want to check satisfiability, is as follows:
```
(and (exists ((x1 S1) ... (xn Sn)) p(x1,...,xn)) 
     (not (exists ((x1 S1) ... (xm Sm)) q(x1,...,xm))))
```
or simply 
```
p(x1,...,xn) 
```
without quantification

where the Si may be Bool, an enumeration Sort, Real or Integer and p and q are formulas containing:

* inequalities between sums of numeric values and constants
* equalities between enumeration values and constants
* boolean values and constants.
* combinations thereof (conjunctions, disjunctions, negations)
However, the formulas may either contain integers or reals but not both.

In order to check satisfiability of quantified formulas, I create a solver using the tactic:

```
(or-else (try-for smt x) (then qe smt))
```
The reason for this choice of solver is that some formulas are simple and therefore fast to check and do not require quantifier elimination, but the plain smt solver fails for some without quantifier elimination. Since it may take very long for the smt solver to fail, I use try-for with a timeout of x.

Now the problem is that depending on the value of x, this tactic produces segmentation faults after a few hundred satisfiability checks (the actual number varies). I have also observed that larger values tend to be safer, but this depends on the complexity of the formulas p and q. Currently, I am using values in the range from 70 to 200.

Another problem is that the tactic returns wrong results after a number of checks. I was able to find a formula for which the solver produced the wrong result regardless of the value of x, i.e. it returned satisfiable instead of unsatisfiable. However, it only produces wrong results if other queries had been made before, but it works correctly if only a single check is performed (see here: http://rise4fun.com/Z3/YslqXo).

As requested on stackoverflow, I provide interaction logs for both of these two issues under the following links: 
* http://www.student.tugraz.at/martin.tappler/tryfor_seg_fault.log.zip
* http://www.student.tugraz.at/martin.tappler/tryfor_wrong_result.log.zip

Unfortunately, they are pretty large, as I have not yet been able to find a smaller example, which shows similar behaviour.

I do not know if this is helpful, but the crash report provided by the JVM lists 
`C  [libz3.so+0x4aec70]  smt::mf::quantifier_analyzer::process_literal(expr*, bool)+0x40`
as the problematic frame after a segmentation fault.

As I did some more experiments with tactics, I tried using the tactic qe-sat, but a solver built using this tactic produces different results than e.g. a solver built using `(then qe smt)`. I did not identify yet, why this happens, because the computation history is again relevant. This means that If I take some formula, for which the results of both solvers differ and I create "fresh" solvers both will produce the same result, but after a number of satisfiability check they will produce different results. 
I observed this behaviour while creating symbolic execution trees and the tree structure was different, when qe-sat was used. 

As before, I recorded interaction logs using com.microsoft.z3.Log. 
* http://www.student.tugraz.at/martin.tappler/qe_sat.log.zip
* http://www.student.tugraz.at/martin.tappler/then_qe_smt.log.zip
Both logs were created using the exact same input data but different tactics for creating solvers. 

I hope the provided information is helpful, otherwise I can provide more information or again try to find a simpler example.

issue_title: Nonlinear arithmetic and quantifiers - bug?
issue_body:
Here is a Z3 query that produces "sat" as result. (I am running Z3 Version 4.8.0, the results are identical in the rise4fun web interface.)

```
(assert (forall ((x Real))
        (exists ((y Real))
                (and (<= 0.0 y) (<= y 1.0) (<= x (* y y))))))
(check-sat)
```
However, this formula should be unsatisfiable! Not every real number is smaller or equal than the square of a number between 0 and 1.

The result changes if I reorder the formulas in the conjunction:

```
(assert (forall ((x Real))
        (exists ((y Real))
                (and (<= x (* y y)) (<= 0.0 y) (<= y 1.0)))))
(check-sat)
```
Then I get "unsat", which is fine.

If I switch on proof generation, then I obtain "unknown", which is at least sound.

```
(set-option :produce-proofs true)

(assert (forall ((x Real))
        (exists ((y Real))
                (and (<= 0.0 y) (<= y 1.0) (<= x (* y y))))))
(check-sat)
```
Is this a bug?

issue_title: Close execution based on other events
issue_body:
Our epic for execution will kill a cell's current execution off if we get an `ABORT_EXECUTION` action with the matching id (as well as if a new execution happens). There are other actions/events where we should expect to close those observables (and not update the display, etc.) It would be really good if this was tracked by the observable (we have the mechanics!) rather than endless bookkeeping all over the place. I'll go ahead and list the actions I'm thinking of and whether we handle that already:
- [x] `ABORT_EXECUTION`
- [x] `EXECUTE_CELL` (a new execution request for an ID comes in)
- [x] `NEW_KERNEL`
- [x] `KILL_KERNEL`
- [x] ~~`INTERRUPT_KERNEL`~~ Nope, the kernel ends up sending back an appropriate message after interrupt that we want to display
- [x] `REMOVE_CELL` #617

Special cases I'm unsure of:
- [ ] `UNDO`

issue_title: Store kernel specs on startup
issue_body:
Currently `kernelspecs.findAll()` is called multiple times throughout the code base. (on startup, on menu init, on kernel launch via spawnteract).

Since it is a promise I think we should only call it once (on startup)  and store the kernel specs in a variable which then can be used throughout the code base.

This should improve performance everywhere kernel specs are used.
Also it would make it possible to very easily add additional kernel specs of bundled kernels in one central place.

E.g. [`ikernel`](https://github.com/rgbkrk/ikernel) could be simply bundled (as a npm dependency) by adding the following spec on startup:

```json
{
  "argv": [
    "path to executable, accessible with `process.execPath`",
    "path to node_modules/ikernel",
    "{connection_file}"
  ],
  "display_name": "ikernel (Node.js)",
  "language": "javascript",
  "env": {
    "ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE": 1
  }
}
```

issue_title: Refactor seems to have caused issues when files exist but in the wrong format
issue_body:
On my system, angr-doc tests fail because they attempt to load an arm binary, depending on libc.so. My machine has `/usr/arm-linux-gnueabi/lib/libc.so`, which CLE finds and attempt so load, but it can't be loaded since it's an ldscript, not an actual elf. When `identify_object` fails to find the backend, it raises a `CLECompatibilityError`, which is never caught and causes the program to halt.

The correct behavior is that the loader continues to search for a valid libc.so, and if none is found, the dependency goes unresolved (contingent on the value of `except_missing_libs`)

I can't assign you for some reason, but this is the job of @subwire.

issue_title: Multiinterpretation of all facts attributes
issue_body:
Возможно ли распарсить одно слово проинтерпретировав его фактом с несколькими активными полями?
**Пример:** хочется находить различные породы собак, а на выходе получать документ,откуда можно будет собирать какую-то статистику.

```
Breed = fact('Breed',
    ['weight','hight','origin','life span'])
```
Пока, я так понимаю, есть вариант писать парсер для каждой такой породы, например
```
BreedCorgi = fact('Breed',
    [attribute('weight', 15.5), attribute('hight', 29.5), attribute(' origin', 'Wales'), attribute('life span', 12)])
CORGI = rule(caseless('корги')).interpretation(BreedCorgi)
```
Но что делать когда словарь таких пород растет, есть ли какой-то более гуманный способ?
Функция интерпретации, насколько можно судить из справочника, не позволяет одно слово интерпретировать по-разному -- получается, что , если встречается слово "корги", то  путем интерпретации и присвоению констаты можно обновить только один атрибут.(т.к. любая новая интерпретацию будет перезаписывать старую)

issue_title: Jobs[] not working as expected
issue_body:
The SpecialNames test is failing at the moment on 6.1.17

https://ganga.web.cern.ch/ganga/release/6.1.17-pre/reports/latest/html/summary_Ganga.test.XMLRepository.html

I don't think this is worth holding the release up, but I suspect this could mean we have potentially some unfortunate problems with RegistrySlices which could plague us in 6.1.17

We should decide if we're planning to drop support for special names or to propagate them correctly as we used to in 6.1.16 and prior. (even if we weren't doing it correctly).

This potentially stops users from doing things such as jobs[5:8] which I'm surprised doesn't cause problems already.

issue_title: Langpacks uploaded via pulp-admin definition on command line
issue_body:
You can also upload a package langpacks definition as a generic unit. You define it with pulp-admin such as:

```
pulp-admin rpm repo uploads langpacks -i gorilla-%s -n gorilla-en -i zebra-%s -n zebra-docs --repo-id zoo
```

You can verify it's added to the repo with:

```
pulp-admin rpm repo list --repo-id zoo
```

You can verify it's defined correctly with:

```
pulp-admin rpm repo content langpacks --repo-id zoo
```

issue_title: Drop API helpers from `pulp_smash.utils`
issue_body:
There is an odd collection of helper functions in module `pulp_smash.utils`, such as `get`, `delete`, `get_importers`, `get_distributors`, `handle_response` and so on. They talk to the Pulp API and nominally help by abstracting away details such as URLs and response checking. Unfortunately, they don't do a great job at this. Let's replace them with a new API client that's better suited to the job: `pulp_smash.api.Client`. More details on this below.

The following modules make use of API helper functions and should be reworked:

* [ ] `pulp_smash.tests.docker.api_v2.test_crud`
* [ ] `pulp_smash.tests.platform.api_v2.test_content_applicability`
* [ ] `pulp_smash.tests.platform.api_v2.test_login`
* [ ] `pulp_smash.tests.platform.api_v2.test_repository`
* [ ] `pulp_smash.tests.platform.api_v2.test_search`
* [ ] `pulp_smash.tests.platform.api_v2.test_user`
* [ ] `pulp_smash.tests.puppet.api_v2.test_sync_publish`
* [ ] `pulp_smash.tests.rpm.api_v2.test_iso_crud`
* [ ] `pulp_smash.tests.rpm.api_v2.test_sync_publish`

What is this `pulp_smash.api.Client` that I speak of? Why use it in particular? The next comment is a copy of the commit message that introduces the client.

issue_title: Check that 1d-notation is supported with LC Predictor
Unpinned dependency on graphql-core leads to issues
issue_body:
We use `graphene` 1.4.1 and haven't yet updated to the recently released 2.0. Because the setup.py [only specifies a minimum version for `graphql-core`](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene/blob/v1.4.1/setup.py#L85), a recent deploy pulled in `graphql-core` 2.0 with `graphene` 1.4.1, versions which are incompatible and resulted in breaking changes.

This is related to a similar issue raised in `graphene-django` (https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/issues/277).

Would you be willing to cut a 1.4.2 release that pinned `graphql-core` to `<2`?

issue_title: Path to 0.5.0
issue_body:
Version `0.5.0` compatible with the `GraphQL` Apr 2016 spec.
*Related Pull Requests:* #54 #59 

## Features
* [x] Improve error handling #41 
* [x] Add ability to add custom errors (inheriting from `GraphQLError`)
* [x] Expose all the function and classes
* [x] Move modules from `graphql.core.*` to `graphql.*`
* [x] Make tests relative to the directories
* [x] ~~Isolate `promise` package into own package in PyPI~~ `pypromise` ☺️

## Breaking changes
Improve the executor to be closer to the reference implementation:

* [x] Uses `Promise` instead of `Deferred` as it simplifies the implementation. Use parallel resolvers automatically as default behavior when available. Related PR: #59 

## Commit Checklist

Implement the [commits from GraphQL-js](https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commits/master?page=3):
* [x] Allow edit nodes on enter and on leave: https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/ecfdb3bb3e90755a7bff6155af50591d52e75625 + https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/d2c005afc87353eceadc03592e74bd6e44063e8e

* [x] [RFC] Proposed change to directive location introspection: https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/e89c19d2ce584f924c2e0e472a61bb805ce260d4
* [x] Fix error message for missing operation + tests: https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/debf3d1fbffc6b31e3b8425a28d3478d8cc0be78 https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/3278e861837cd3f7d17eaec54f3f04b175300826
* [x] [RFC] Directives in schema language: https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/fdafe32724f2d6dac1ba360f1a440c0e633e75d9
* [x] Updating schema parser to more closely match current state of RFC: https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/pull/323/commits/b0885a038ec0e654962d69fb910ac86659279579
* [x] [RFC] Add Schema Definition to IDL https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/8379e71f7011fe044574f4bdef2a761d18d6cf2c
* [x] Remove unused function parameters https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/43992e3a60d7bf8f5e7d2f29dfae302b5ba72eec
* [x] Include test for extending a schema that uses Enums https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/37924d240d7e094382927e4f612d61ff7dda3cb3
* [x] Cache client schema exe function, and include test for unreferenced interface https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/1083c7e0f3dbd6be801a12f688fe0959716c54ac
* [x] Naming similarity https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/3f6a7f4ca9cd9242819e0e14fc7916635fbeeced
* [x] Move getTypeOf to execute.js and rename to defaultResolveTypeFn to mirror defaultResolveFn https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/edc405a11508a110759ce53c9efb2eb6dd2d181c + https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/a781b556eea66bbf3cd82e438f560be9d9bec22c
* [x] Add tests and refine default resolve function. https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/d506c23e413a0b2d5b6b169caca4af928996d1d9
* [x] Add GraphQLSchema types field https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/6a1f23e1f9c1e6bf4cea837bc9bb6eae0fb5c382 + Fix test https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/09be751e995dd641f138f67ff9f420525950fad1

* [x] Improve coercion error messages https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/dea5aacca01f4429026bea3d97ea7cbc88b33bcf + Minor touch ups: https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/commit/136630f8fd8778c4d2e61d07c0b1ec47f99c9bf6

issue_title: Allow for custom package root path
issue_body:
Currently, `ConanMultiPackager._docker_pack` method has path to "project" directory hard-coded:
```python
command = "sudo docker run --rm -v %s:/home/conan/project -v " \
          "~/.conan/data:/home/conan/.conan/data -it %s %s /bin/sh -c \"" \
          "cd project && sudo pip install conan_package_tools --upgrade %s && " \
          "conan_json_packager\"" % (curdir, env_vars, image_name, specific_conan_package)
```

I would like to be able to use custom path (e.g. subdirectory of "project"), maybe via another `CONAN_...` environment variable. This would allow for multiple packages to be stored within a single repository.

issue_title: Add API level support for an insecure connection.
issue_body: Practical device pairing can depend on the ability of a new device to communicate to one or more other members of a PAN without the security key.  This would be with the aid of an insecure port setup.  However to achieve this functionality, there needs to exist a set of OT api's that allows a new device to bring up the wireless interface without a security key.

issue_title: Ensure custom error types are open source
issue_body: We need to check each library for whether it has custom error types that are not present in the open source version. I believe a previous version of the public proto generator did not support custom errors. It does now, so we should update the relevant libraries.

Hello,

I'm adapting the CIFAR-10 example to my own case.
My dataset is around 30k images spread out across 11 classes. Although not equally spread out, as per frequency of each class: [5.4, 13.0, 13.6, 13.7, 10.5, 7.6, 2.0, 5.2, 19.0, 5.1, 5.0] %

From this dataset I randomly picked 15% of the image as my validation set and also set aside another 5% for prediction after training.

```
Training Step: 3336  | total loss: 0.64427
| Adam | epoch: 008 | loss: 0.64427 - acc: 0.7864 | val_loss: 0.73456 - val_acc: 0.7790 -- iter: 26662/26662
Training Step: 3753  | total loss: 0.58631
| Adam | epoch: 009 | loss: 0.58631 - acc: 0.7945 | val_loss: 0.67474 - val_acc: 0.7892 -- iter: 26662/26662
Training Step: 4170  | total loss: 0.54879
| Adam | epoch: 010 | loss: 0.54879 - acc: 0.8210 | val_loss: 0.64050 - val_acc: 0.7977 -- iter: 26662/26662
```
At the end of training
I load the final validation set and run the following code

```
X_eval, Y_eval , image_names = snaps.load_validation_data(keyword='teams', year=args.year)

results = model.predict(X_eval)

hits = 0
print('Prediction, Real Tag, Image')
for index, line in enumerate(results):
    prediction = np.argmax(line)
    reality = Y_eval[index]
    if prediction == reality:
        hits += 1
    print('%s, %s, %s' % (prediction, reality, image_names[index]))

print('Accuracy: %s' % (float(hits)/len(results)))
```

But I never get accuracy which is more than 0.2, 0.3 because the model seems to predict class 1 80% of the time

This is probably a classic case of over-fitting but I can't understand where the high accuracy values come from since the validation set used should be enough to pick up on this over-fitting while the model runs.

So how is that accuracy number really calculated at the end of each epoch? I couldn't find it in the documentation.

Thanks.

Backporting eval
issue_body:
I am now facing the problem that I need to create some Python object instances from C++. As such, I could really use `py::eval`.

Now, before I move that into our repo I was wondering whether it would make sense to backport `py::eval` to 1.8 and perhaps then make a 1.8.2 on the long run?

BTW, is there any reason why there hasn't been a release while 1.9 seems to get quite huge?

issue_title: SubfieldBase has been removed in Django 1.10
issue_body: Django 1.10 is officially out and breaks django-ldapdb since it removed SubfieldBase, which is used by django_ldapdb/models.

issue_title: requests.exceptions.SSLError: hostname
issue_body:
When I try to use the instructions from the documentation to  connect to a GKE cluster using the config file it fails with the dreaded: "requests.exceptions.SSLError: hostname."   I expect this to work by not validating the hostname and instead validating the cert. 

The workaround is to use kubectl proxy and http://localhost:8001 which is just an extra step for the user. It would be nice if requests could turn host validation off in this case, but there is no such feature in that library.  I filed a [feature request](https://github.com/kennethreitz/requests/issues/3955#issuecomment-291876110) with it as well, but it is basically rejected. 

It may be worth updating the docs to deal with this.

issue_title: Standalone option for running a TLS server when validating HTTP-01 challenges
issue_body:
Boulder will follow insecure redirects when validating a http-01 challenge. Hence, redirects to insecure TLS domains will be followed.

Since the `ServerManager` has all the certificates needed to run a TLS server already, there should be an option to use TLS when validating a HTTP-01 challenge.

issue_title: Handle large result sets
issue_body:
### Problem
Large result sets (100K+) cause `RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded while calling a Python object` due to [this recursion](https://github.com/simple-salesforce/simple-salesforce/blob/master/simple_salesforce/api.py#L407).

### Solution
* Use a non recursive implementation to get results
* Use a generator to process results in small batches rather than holding the entire result set in memory

issue_title: Detect path cycles and don't answer repeated mediated transfer
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

A mediated transfer might loop over the network with the current routing implementation, were each hop is free to choose the next node to which the message will be sent.

## Solution

Detect if the node received an equal message before and do not handle if it has.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Maintain a dictionary that can be used to query if the message is known or not
- [ ] Change the `RaidenMessageHandler` to ingore duplicated transfers.

issue_title: Implement API endpoint for querying connection to token network
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

We have API endpoints to [connect](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/f0d57190aa92258e7e4cf70b6c845719d6bb2121/docs/rest_api.rst#connecting-to-a-token-network) and to [leave](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/f0d57190aa92258e7e4cf70b6c845719d6bb2121/docs/rest_api.rst#leaving-a-token-network) a token network.

Once a user is connected to a token network, a connection manager has been created for that network which manages the user's connections to channels of that network. The problem is that there is no way for the user to tell if such a connection manager has been created once it is there.

## Solution

Create a way for users to query if they are connected (in other words if a ConnectionManager is active) to a token network through the REST API.

The simplest way to achieve this is to add the possibility to perform two new requests to the REST API.

#### Checking existence of connection to a token network

 a `GET` request to the connection endpoint for a token. e.g.:

`GET /api/<version>/connection/<token_address>`

If there is no connection it should return a `204 No content` and if there is a connection a `200 OK` with the allocated funds for the connection manager, much like the `PUT` request does.

#### Getting all connections to token networks.

A `GET` request to:

`GET /api/<version>/connection`

Always returns 200 OK and if there are no connections it should return an empty list, but if there are connections it should return a list of the tokens we have a connection to.

issue_title: Filtering on fields which start with _
issue_body:
Hello,

It appears as if it is not possible to `filter` or `exclude` on fields which start with an `_`. 

For example: `MyPolymorphicModel.objects.filter(related_object___related_object_field="query")` raises `AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'get_model'` (Note: the field on `related_object` is `_related_object_field` with a leading underscore).

Based on the line comment here: https://github.com/django-polymorphic/django-polymorphic/blob/master/polymorphic/query_translate.py#L133, it appears as if the existence of a triple underscore in a filter expression implies that some polymorphic filtering should occur. This is not the case in my example, and it seems very limiting to assume that any query with a triple underscore is attempting to filter for a polymorphic field rather than a field which has a name starting with an underscore.

Thanks for your help!

issue_title: Shorthand (or default) string interpolation for lists
issue_body:
Now that SoS uses Python's f-string method, I find it a bit difficult to properly format variables the way it used to be. For example:

```
[1]
n = [x for x in range(2)]
input: [f'{x+1}.txt' for x in range(2)], group_by = 1, paired_with = 'n'
run: expand = '${ }'
  echo ${_n}
```

Will result in

```
[0]
[1]
````

So I will always have to use `${_n[0]}`. Also things like `${_n:,}`, `${_n:s}` do not work. And if I need the entire list I'll have to do `${' '.join(n)}`. All these are a bit difficult to use. Should we try to make variable string interpolation the way it was, because that'd be more intuitive?

issue_title: Deferred status transition with Lpar.activate() and deactivate()
issue_labels: [{'id': 429586299, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/area:%20code', 'name': 'area: code', 'color': '207de5', 'default': False}, {'id': 429588767, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/resolution:%20fixed', 'name': 'resolution: fixed', 'color': 'cccccc', 'default': False}, {'id': 429587821, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/type:%20feature', 'name': 'type: feature', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
When the `Lpar.activate()` and `deactivate()` methods complete (e.g. by returning after being called with `wait_for_completion=True`), the LPAR state may not have reached the desired state, and a subsequent status retrieval still reports the old state.

This does not happen always, and the exact circumstances are not fully understood at this point.

As a result, users of the zhmcclient have to code around this and loop on status retrieval until the desired state is reached.

The zhmcclient could take care of that by performing the stats polling inside of these methods for the `wait_for_completion=True` case, making life easier for the user. In the `wait_for_completion=False` case, adressing this is more complex, but not impossible, given that we have PR #182: The async method could return a new subclass of `Job` that implements its check and wait methods such that completion includes polling for the desired status.

Discussion points:
* Should we do this at all?
* Should a timeout be added? (see Issue #6)

issue_title: Parallelize resource deletion
issue_body:
Resource deletion is somewhat unique in Pulumi in that we have a complete view of the dependency graph of resources that already exist. When it comes time to delete a resource, we can parallelize execution of `Delete` steps across the dependency DAG of a condemned resource.

Doing so requires us to be intelligent about step dependencies and have a nontrivial task scheduler, so it's a higher-level milestone for parallelism.

issue_title: JSON codec reshapes string arrays
issue_body:
This is carrying on from https://github.com/zarr-developers/zarr/issues/258

I've tried to come up with a minimal example, but it's tricky to illustrate without showing the context. Here is an interaction with zarr with some instrumentation in the encode/decode methods for json.

```python
z = zarr.empty(2, dtype=object, object_codec=numcodecs.JSON(), chunks=(1,))
z[0] = ["11"]
z[1] = ["1", "1"]

print(z[:]) # Borks
```
output:
```
INPUT: (1,)
INPUT: (1,)
OUTPUT: (1, 1)
OUTPUT: (1, 2)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "dev.py", line 34, in <module>
    print(z[:]) # Borks
  File "/home/jk/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/zarr/core.py", line 559, in __getitem__
    return self.get_basic_selection(selection, fields=fields)
  File "/home/jk/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/zarr/core.py", line 685, in get_basic_selection
    fields=fields)
  File "/home/jk/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/zarr/core.py", line 727, in _get_basic_selection_nd
    return self._get_selection(indexer=indexer, out=out, fields=fields)
  File "/home/jk/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/zarr/core.py", line 1015, in _get_selection
    drop_axes=indexer.drop_axes, fields=fields)
  File "/home/jk/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/zarr/core.py", line 1608, in _chunk_getitem
    chunk = self._decode_chunk(cdata)
  File "/home/jk/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/zarr/core.py", line 1751, in _decode_chunk
    chunk = chunk.reshape(self._chunks, order=self._order)
ValueError: cannot reshape array of size 2 into shape (1,)
```
The INPUT lines are the shapes of the input arrays to ``encode`` and the OUTPUT lines are the corresponding output shapes of the arrays from ``decode``.

#### Problem description

When calling ``numpy.array([["s1", "s2"], ["s3, "s4"]], dtype=object)`` numpy is quite aggressive about reshaping the array to store things more efficiently.

I've played around with this a fair bit, and I think the only options are to 

1. Drop the numpy dependency in the encoding and decoding steps for JSON (i.e, don't include the dtype in the JSON encoding), and provide the supplied argument directly to the JSON encoder (and conversely, directly return the value of json.loads() from decode.

2. Also encode the input array shape in the JSON encoding.

Both of these options are ugly because they break backward compatibility. I'll make a PR for demonstrating option 2 in a minute for discussion.

issue_title: This repo seems dead. Alternatives?
issue_body:
No fault on the owner - they created a very useful package which seems to have no better alternative. But I'd like to see development continue on this and would like to contribute if I can.

I see that pull requests have been left unmerged for 2 months and owner seems to not be actively answering questions. At this point we have to consider moving elsewhere if we want to see this continue. Anyone who actually wants to contribute to this will need to find a different way to do so.

Is there already a good candidate fork for moving to which has someone who will actively maintain it?

If not, perhaps I can go through the trouble of tracking down the forks and getting the PRs and other changes merged into my own fork. Actions speak louder than words, of course. But I'd like to opt for someone else's fork if they feel they have a strong grasp on this codebase / area.

issue_title: Exposes "submit" method on form elements
issue_body:
If I have a form, like in:

    from splinter.browser import Browser
    browser = Browser()
    browser.visit('http://www.google.com/')
    my_form = browser.find_by_tag('form').first

the object `my_form._element` will have the `submit` method.

As we should not use `_element` since it is related to Selenium, Splinter should exposes a `submit` method in this form-object, so we can submit forms without needing to search for the submit button (or element that actually submits the form).

It is not useful when we want to test if some button, when clicked, submits the form correctly. But it's very useful when we just want to submit the form and check what is the response (and don't want to know the name of the button/element that submits the form).
